children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
Introduction
In order to prevent AIFIs and other financial institutions from being used as a channel
for Money Laundering (ML) / Terrorist Financing (TF) and to ensure the integrity and
stability of the financial system, efforts are continuously being made both
internationally and nationally, by way of prescribing various rules and regulations.
Internationally, the Financial Action Task Force (FATF), which is an inter-governmental
body established in 1989 by the Ministers of its member jurisdictions, sets standards
and promotes effective implementation of legal, regulatory and operational measures
for combating money laundering, terrorist financing and other related threats to the
integrity of the international financial system. India, as a member of FATF, is
committed to upholding measures to protect the integrity of the international financial
system.
In India, the Prevention of Money-Laundering Act, 2002, and the Prevention of Money-
Laundering (Maintenance of Records) Rules, 2005, form the legal framework on Anti-
Money Laundering (AML) and Countering the Financing of Terrorism (CFT). The
provisions of the PML Act, 2002 and the PML Rules, 2005, as amended from time to
time by the Government of India, require Regulated Entities (REs) to follow certain
customer identification procedures while undertaking a transaction either by
establishing an account-based relationship or otherwise, and to monitor their
transactions.
Accordingly, in exercise of the powers conferred by sections 35A of the Banking
Regulation Act, 1949, the Banking Regulation Act (AACS), 1949, read with section 56
of the Act ibid., sections 45JA, 45K, and 45L of the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934,
section 10(2) read with section 18 of Payment and Settlement Systems Act 2007 (Act
51 of 2007), section 11(1) of the Foreign Exchange Management Act, 1999, Rule 9(14)
of the Prevention of Money-Laundering (Maintenance of Records) Rules, 2005, and
all other laws enabling the Reserve Bank in this regard, the RBI being satisfied that it
is necessary and expedient in the public interest so to do, hereby issues the Directions
hereinafter specified.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: A. Short Title and Commencement
children.text:
A. Short Title and Commencement
1.
These Directions shall be called the Reserve Bank of India (All India Financial
Institutions - Know Your Customer) Directions, 2025.
2.
These directions shall come into force with immediate effect

children.title: B. Applicability
children.text:
B. Applicability
3.
These Directions shall be applicable to All-India Financial Institutions (hereinafter
collectively referred to as 'AIFIs' and individually as an 'AIFI') viz. Export Import
Bank of India ('EXIM Bank'), National Bank for Agriculture and Rural
Development ('NABARD'), Small Industries Development Bank of India ('SIDBI'),
National Housing Bank ('NHB') and National Bank for Financing Infrastructure
and Development ('NaBFID').
4.
These directions shall also apply to those branches and majority-owned
subsidiaries of the AIFI which are located abroad, to the extent they are not
contradictory to the local laws in the host country, provided that:
(1) where applicable laws and regulations prohibit implementation of these
guidelines, the AIFI shall bring the same to the notice of the RBI. The RBI
may advise the AIFI to take further necessary action, including application
of additional measures to manage the ML / TF risks.
(2) in case there is a variance in KYC / AML standards prescribed by the RBI
and the host country regulators, branches / subsidiaries of the AIFI shall
adopt the more stringent regulation of the two.

children.title: C. Definitions
children.text:
C. Definitions
5.
In these Directions, unless the context otherwise requires, the following
meanings are assigned to the terms herein:
(1) Terms bearing meaning assigned in terms of the Prevention of Money-
Laundering Act, 2002, and the Prevention of Money-Laundering
(Maintenance of Records) Rules, 2005:

children.title: (i) 'Aadhaar number' shall have the meaning assigned to it in clause (a)
children.text:
(i) 'Aadhaar number' shall have the meaning assigned to it in clause (a)
of section 2 of the Aadhaar (Targeted Delivery of Financial and Other

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
Subsidies, Benefits and Services) Act, 2016 (18 of 2016);

children.title: (ii) 'Act' and 'Rules' mean the Prevention of Money-Laundering Act,
children.text:
(ii) 'Act' and 'Rules' mean the Prevention of Money-Laundering Act,
2002 and the Prevention of Money-Laundering (Maintenance of
Records) Rules, 2005, respectively and amendments thereto.

children.title: (iii) 'Authentication', in the context of Aadhaar authentication, means the
children.text:
(iii) 'Authentication', in the context of Aadhaar authentication, means the
process as defined under sub-section (c) of section 2 of the Aadhaar
(Targeted Delivery of Financial and Other Subsidies, Benefits and
Services) Act, 2016.

children.title: (iv) Beneficial Owner (BO)
children.text: (iv) Beneficial Owner (BO)

children.title: (a) Where the customer is a company, the beneficial owner is the
children.text:
(a) Where the customer is a company, the beneficial owner is the
natural person(s), who, whether acting alone or together, or
through one or more juridical persons, has / have a controlling
ownership interest or who exercises control through other
means.

children.title: Explanation: For the purpose of this sub-clause-
children.text: Explanation: For the purpose of this sub-clause-

children.title: • 'Controlling ownership interest' means ownership of /
children.text:
• 'Controlling ownership interest' means ownership of /
entitlement to more than 10 percent of the shares or capital
or profits of the company.

children.title: • 'Control' shall include the right to appoint the majority of the
children.text:
• 'Control' shall include the right to appoint the majority of the
directors or to control the management or policy decisions
including by virtue of their shareholding or management
rights or shareholders agreements or voting agreements.

children.title: (b) Where the customer is a partnership firm, the beneficial owner
children.text:
(b) Where the customer is a partnership firm, the beneficial owner
is the natural person(s), who, whether acting alone or together,
or through one or more juridical person(s), has / have ownership
of / entitlement to more than 10 percent of capital or profits of the
partnership or who exercises control through other means.

children.title: Explanation: For the purpose of this sub-clause, 'control' shall
children.text:
Explanation: For the purpose of this sub-clause, 'control' shall
include the right to control the management or policy decision.

children.title: (c) Where the customer is an unincorporated association or
children.text:
(c) Where the customer is an unincorporated association or
body of individuals, the beneficial owner is the natural

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
person(s), who, whether acting alone or together, or through one
or more juridical person, has / have ownership of / entitlement to
more than 15 percent of the property or capital or profits of the
unincorporated association or body of individuals.

children.title: Explanation: Term 'body of individuals' includes societies.
children.text:
Explanation: Term 'body of individuals' includes societies.
Where no natural person is identified under (a), (b) or (c) above,
the beneficial owner is the relevant natural person who holds the
position of senior managing official.

children.title: (d) Where the customer is a trust, the identification of beneficial
children.text:
(d) Where the customer is a trust, the identification of beneficial
owner(s) shall include identification of the author of the trust, the
trustee, the beneficiaries with 10 percent or more interest in the
trust and any other natural person exercising ultimate effective
control over the trust through a chain of control or ownership.

children.title: (v) 'Certified Copy' - Obtaining the certified copy by the AIFI shall mean
children.text:
(v) 'Certified Copy' - Obtaining the certified copy by the AIFI shall mean
comparing the copy of the proof of possession of Aadhaar number
where offline verification cannot be carried out or the officially valid
document produced by the customer with the original, and an
authorised officer of the AIFI shall record the comparison on the copy as
per the provisions contained in the Act. Provided that in case of Non-
Resident Indians (NRIs) and Persons of Indian Origin (PIOs), as
defined in Foreign Exchange Management (Deposit) Regulations,
2016 {FEMA 5(R)}, the AIFI may alternatively obtain the original
certified copy, certified by any one of the following:

children.title: (a) authorised officials of overseas branches of Scheduled
children.text:
(a) authorised officials of overseas branches of Scheduled
Commercial Banks registered in India,

children.title: (b) branches of overseas banks with whom Indian banks have
children.text:
(b) branches of overseas banks with whom Indian banks have
relationships,

children.title: (c) Notary Public abroad,
children.text: (c) Notary Public abroad,

children.title: (d) Court Magistrate,
children.text: (d) Court Magistrate,

children.title: (e) Judge,
children.text: (e) Judge,

children.title: (f) Indian Embassy / Consulate General in the country where the
children.text: (f) Indian Embassy / Consulate General in the country where the

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
non-resident customer resides.

children.title: (vi) 'Central KYC Records Registry' (CKYCR) means an entity defined
children.text:
(vi) 'Central KYC Records Registry' (CKYCR) means an entity defined
under Rule 2(1) of the Rules, to receive, store, safeguard and retrieve
the KYC records in digital form of a customer.

children.title: (vii) 'Designated Director' means a person whom the AIFI designates to
children.text:
(vii) 'Designated Director' means a person whom the AIFI designates to
ensure overall compliance with the obligations imposed under

hierarchy.title: chapter IV of the PML Act and the Rules and shall include the
hierarchy.text:
chapter IV of the PML Act and the Rules and shall include the
Managing Director or a whole-time Director, whom the Board of
Directors has duly authorised.

children.title: Explanation: For the purpose of this clause, the terms 'Managing
children.text:
Explanation: For the purpose of this clause, the terms 'Managing
Director' and 'Whole-time Director' shall have the meaning assigned
to them in the Companies Act, 2013.

children.title: (viii) 'Digital KYC' means that an authorised officer of the AIFI captures a
children.text:
(viii) 'Digital KYC' means that an authorised officer of the AIFI captures a
live photo of the customer and officially valid document or the proof
of possession of Aadhaar (where offline verification cannot be carried
out), along with the latitude and longitude of the location where
such live photo is being taken, as per the provisions contained in the
Act.

children.title: (ix) 'Digital Signature' shall have the same meaning as assigned to it in
children.text:
(ix) 'Digital Signature' shall have the same meaning as assigned to it in
clause (p) of sub-section (1) of section (2) of the Information
Technology Act, 2000 (21 of 2000).

children.title: (x) 'Equivalent e-document' means an electronic equivalent of a
children.text:
(x) 'Equivalent e-document' means an electronic equivalent of a
document that the issuing authority of such document issues with
its valid digital signature, including documents issued to the digital
locker account of the customer as per rule 9 of the Information
Technology (Preservation and Retention of Information by
Intermediaries Providing Digital Locker Facilities) Rules, 2016.

children.title: (xi) 'Group' - The term 'group' shall have the same meaning assigned
children.text:
(xi) 'Group' - The term 'group' shall have the same meaning assigned
to it in clause (e) of sub-section (9) of section 286 of the Income-tax
Act,1961 (43 of 1961).

children.title: (xii) 'Know Your Client (KYC) Identifier' means the unique number or code
children.text: (xii) 'Know Your Client (KYC) Identifier' means the unique number or code

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
that the Central KYC Records Registry assigns to a customer.

children.title: Explanation: A customer can obtain his KYC Identifier through the
children.text:
Explanation: A customer can obtain his KYC Identifier through the
following ways: In the process of opening an account, once the
customer's KYC Identifier is generated by CKYCR and provided to the
AIFI, the latter shall share the same with the concerned customer. The
customer can also access his KYC Identifier on CKYCR Portal
(www.ckycindia.in).

children.title: (xiii) 'Non-profit organisations' (NPO) means any entity or organisation,
children.text:
(xiii) 'Non-profit organisations' (NPO) means any entity or organisation,
constituted for religious or charitable purposes referred to in clause (15)
of section 2 of the Income-tax Act, 1961 (43 of 1961), that is registered
as a trust or a society under the Societies Registration Act, 1860 or any
similar State legislation or a company registered under section 8 of the
Companies Act, 2013 (18 of 2013).

children.title: (xiv) 'Officially Valid Document' (OVD) means the passport, the driving
children.text:
(xiv) 'Officially Valid Document' (OVD) means the passport, the driving
licence, proof of possession of Aadhaar number, the Voter's Identity Card
that the Election Commission of India issues, the job card that NREGA
issues and an officer of the State Government duly signs, and the letter
that the National Population Register issues containing details of name
and address.
Provided that,

children.title: (a) where the customer submits his proof of possession of Aadhaar
children.text:
(a) where the customer submits his proof of possession of Aadhaar
number as an OVD, he may submit it in such form that the Unique
Identification Authority of India (UIDAI) issues.

children.title: (b) When the customer furnishes an OVD that does not have an updated
children.text:
(b) When the customer furnishes an OVD that does not have an updated
address, the AIFI shall deem the following documents or the
equivalent e-documents thereof to be OVDs for the limited purpose of
proof of address:-
i.
utility bill which is not more than two months old of any service
provider (electricity, telephone, post-paid mobile phone, piped
gas, water bill);
ii.
property or Municipal tax receipt;

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
iii.
pension or family pension payment orders (PPOs) issued to
retired employees by Government Departments or Public
Sector Undertakings, if they contain the address;
iv.
letter of allotment of accommodation from employer that is
issued by State Government or Central Government
Departments, statutory or regulatory bodies, public sector
undertakings, scheduled commercial banks, financial
institutions and listed companies and leave and licence
agreements with such employers allotting official
accommodation;
Illustration: If a customer is staying in Chennai but their OVD
contains an address of New Delhi, they can open an account
in Chennai by submitting a deemed to be OVD for the
purpose of proof of address. However, as mentioned below in
clause (c), they are required to submit an OVD with current
address within a period of three months.

children.title: (c) the customer shall submit OVD with current address within a period
children.text:
(c) the customer shall submit OVD with current address within a period
of three months of submitting the documents specified at (b) above

children.title: (d) if the OVD that a foreign national present does not contain the
children.text:
(d) if the OVD that a foreign national present does not contain the
details of address, the AIFI shall accept documents that
Government departments of foreign jurisdictions issue, and a letter
that the Foreign Embassy or Mission in India issues, as proof of
address.

children.title: Explanation: For the purpose of this clause, the AIFI shall deem a
children.text:
Explanation: For the purpose of this clause, the AIFI shall deem a
document to be an OVD even if there is a change in the name
subsequent to its issuance provided that it is supported by a
marriage certificate that the State Government issues or a Gazette
notification, indicating such a change of name.

children.title: (xv) 'Offline verification' shall have the same meaning as assigned to it in
children.text:
(xv) 'Offline verification' shall have the same meaning as assigned to it in
clause (pa) of section 2 of the Aadhaar (Targeted Delivery of Financial
and Other Subsidies, Benefits and Services) Act, 2016 (18 of 2016).

children.title: (xvi) 'Person' has the same meaning assigned in the Act and includes:
children.text: (xvi) 'Person' has the same meaning assigned in the Act and includes:

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (a) an individual,
children.text: (a) an individual,

children.title: (b) a Hindu undivided family,
children.text: (b) a Hindu undivided family,

children.title: (c) a company,
children.text: (c) a company,

children.title: (d) a firm,
children.text: (d) a firm,

children.title: (e) an association of persons or a body of individuals, whether
children.text:
(e) an association of persons or a body of individuals, whether
incorporated or not,

children.title: (f) every artificial juridical person, not falling within any one of the
children.text:
(f) every artificial juridical person, not falling within any one of the
above persons (a to e), and

children.title: (g) any agency, office or branch owned or controlled by any of the
children.text:
(g) any agency, office or branch owned or controlled by any of the
above persons (a to f).

children.title: (xvii) 'Principal Officer' means a AIFI's nominated officer at the
children.text:
(xvii) 'Principal Officer' means a AIFI's nominated officer at the
management level, responsible for furnishing information as per rule 8
of the Rules.

children.title: (xviii) 'Suspicious transaction' means a 'transaction' as defined below,
children.text:
(xviii) 'Suspicious transaction' means a 'transaction' as defined below,
including an attempted transaction, whether or not made in cash,
which, to a person acting in good faith:

children.title: (a) gives rise to a reasonable ground of suspicion that it may involve
children.text:
(a) gives rise to a reasonable ground of suspicion that it may involve
proceeds of an offence specified in the Schedule to the Act,
regardless of the value involved; or

children.title: (b) appears to be made in circumstances of unusual or unjustified
children.text:
(b) appears to be made in circumstances of unusual or unjustified
complexity; or

children.title: (c) appears to have no economic rationale or bona fide purpose; or
children.text: (c) appears to have no economic rationale or bona fide purpose; or

children.title: (d) gives rise to a reasonable ground of suspicion that it may involve
children.text:
(d) gives rise to a reasonable ground of suspicion that it may involve
financing of the activities relating to terrorism.

children.title: Explanation: Transaction involving financing of the activities
children.text:
Explanation: Transaction involving financing of the activities
relating to terrorism includes transaction involving funds that the
AIFI suspects are linked or related to, or to be used for terrorism,
terrorist acts or by a terrorist, terrorist organisation or those who
finance or are attempting to finance terrorism.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (xix) 'Transaction' means a purchase, sale, loan, pledge, gift, transfer,
children.text:
(xix) 'Transaction' means a purchase, sale, loan, pledge, gift, transfer,
delivery or the arrangement thereof and includes:

children.title: (a) opening of an account;
children.text: (a) opening of an account;

children.title: (b) deposit, withdrawal, exchange or transfer of funds in whatever
(c) the use of a safety deposit box or any other form of safe deposit;
children.text: (c) the use of a safety deposit box or any other form of safe deposit;

children.title: (d) entering into any fiduciary relationship;
children.text: (d) entering into any fiduciary relationship;

children.title: (e) any payment made or received, in whole or in part, for any
children.text:
(e) any payment made or received, in whole or in part, for any
contractual or other legal obligation; or

children.title: (f) establishing or creating a legal person or legal arrangement.
children.text:
(f) establishing or creating a legal person or legal arrangement.
(2) Unless the context otherwise requires, terms in these Directions shall bear
the meanings assigned to them below:

children.title: (i) 'Common Reporting Standards' (CRS) means reporting standards
children.text:
(i) 'Common Reporting Standards' (CRS) means reporting standards
set for implementation of multilateral agreement signed to automatically
exchange information based on Article 6 of the Convention on Mutual
Administrative Assistance in Tax Matters.

children.title: (ii) Correspondent Banking: Correspondent banking is the provision of
children.text:
(ii) Correspondent Banking: Correspondent banking is the provision of
banking services by one bank (the 'correspondent bank') to another
bank (the 'respondent bank'). A correspondent bank may provide the
respondent banks with a wide range of services, including cash
management (e.g., interest-bearing accounts in a variety of currencies),
international wire transfers, cheque clearing, payable- through
accounts and foreign exchange services.

children.title: (iii) 'Customer' means a person who is engaged in a financial transaction
children.text:
(iii) 'Customer' means a person who is engaged in a financial transaction
or activity with the AIFI and includes a person on whose behalf the
person who is engaged in the transaction or activity, is acting.

children.title: (iv) 'Walk-in Customer' means a person who does not have an account-
children.text:
(iv) 'Walk-in Customer' means a person who does not have an account-
based relationship with the AIFI, but undertakes transactions with the
AIFI.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (v) 'Customer Due Diligence (CDD)' means identifying and verifying the
children.text:
(v) 'Customer Due Diligence (CDD)' means identifying and verifying the
customer and the beneficial owner using reliable and independent
sources of identification.

children.title: Explanation: The CDD, at the time of commencement of an account-
children.text:
Explanation: The CDD, at the time of commencement of an account-
based relationship or while carrying out occasional transaction of an
amount equal to or exceeding ₹50,000, whether conducted as a single
transaction or several transactions that appear to be connected, or any
international money transfer operations, shall include:

children.title: (a) Identification of the customer, verification of their identity using
children.text:
(a) Identification of the customer, verification of their identity using
reliable and independent sources of identification, obtaining
information on the purpose and intended nature of the business
relationship, where applicable

children.title: (b) Taking reasonable steps to understand the nature of the customer's
children.text:
(b) Taking reasonable steps to understand the nature of the customer's
business, and its ownership and control;

children.title: (c) Determining whether a customer is acting on behalf of a beneficial
children.text:
(c) Determining whether a customer is acting on behalf of a beneficial
owner, and identifying the beneficial owner and taking all steps to
verify the identity of the beneficial owner, using reliable and
independent sources of identification.

children.title: (vi) 'Customer identification' means undertaking the process of CDD.
children.text: (vi) 'Customer identification' means undertaking the process of CDD.

children.title: (vii) 'FATCA' means Foreign Account Tax Compliance Act of the United
children.text:
(vii) 'FATCA' means Foreign Account Tax Compliance Act of the United
States of America (USA) which, inter alia, requires foreign financial
institutions to report about financial accounts held by U.S. taxpayers or
foreign entities in which U.S. taxpayers hold a substantial ownership
interest.

children.title: (viii) 'IGA' means Inter Governmental Agreement between the Governments
children.text:
(viii) 'IGA' means Inter Governmental Agreement between the Governments
of India and the USA to improve international tax compliance and to
implement FATCA of the USA.

children.title: (ix) 'KYC Templates' means templates prepared to facilitate collating and
children.text:
(ix) 'KYC Templates' means templates prepared to facilitate collating and
reporting KYC data to the CKYCR, for individuals and legal entities.

children.title: (x) 'Non-face-to-face customers' means customers who open accounts
children.text:
(x) 'Non-face-to-face customers' means customers who open accounts
without visiting the branch / offices of the AIFI or meeting the officials of

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
the AIFI.

children.title: (xi) 'On-going Due Diligence' means regular monitoring of transactions in
children.text:
(xi) 'On-going Due Diligence' means regular monitoring of transactions in
accounts to ensure that transactions are consistent with the AIFI's
knowledge about the customers, customers' business and risk profile,
the source of funds / wealth.

children.title: (xii) Payable-through accounts: The term payable-through accounts
children.text:
(xii) Payable-through accounts: The term payable-through accounts
refers to correspondent accounts that third parties use directly to
transact business on their own behalf.

children.title: (xiii) 'Periodic Updation' means the steps taken to ensure that documents,
children.text:
(xiii) 'Periodic Updation' means the steps taken to ensure that documents,
data or information collected under the CDD process are kept up-to-
date and relevant by undertaking reviews of existing records at the
periodicity prescribed by the RBI.

children.title: (xiv) 'Shell Bank' means a bank that has no physical presence in the
children.text:
(xiv) 'Shell Bank' means a bank that has no physical presence in the
country in which it is incorporated and licensed, and which is unaffiliated
with a regulated financial group that is subject to effective consolidated
supervision. Physical presence means meaningful mind and
management located within a country. The existence simply of a local
agent or low-level staff does not constitute physical presence.

children.title: (xv) 'Regulated Entities' (REs) means:
children.text: (xv) 'Regulated Entities' (REs) means:

children.title: (a) all Scheduled Commercial Banks (SCBs) / Regional Rural Banks
children.text:
(a) all Scheduled Commercial Banks (SCBs) / Regional Rural Banks
(RRBs) / Local Area Banks (LABs) / All Primary (Urban) Co-
operative Banks (UCBs) / State and Central Co-operative Banks
(StCBs / CCBs), and any other entity which has been licensed
under section 22 of Banking Regulation Act, 1949, which as a
group shall be referred as 'banks'

children.title: (b) All India Financial Institutions (AIFIs)
children.text: (b) All India Financial Institutions (AIFIs)

children.title: (c) All Non-Banking Finance Companies (NBFCs), Miscellaneous
children.text:
(c) All Non-Banking Finance Companies (NBFCs), Miscellaneous
Non-Banking Companies (MNBCs) and Residuary Non-Banking
Companies (RNBCs)

children.title: (d) Asset Reconstruction Companies (ARCs)
children.text: (d) Asset Reconstruction Companies (ARCs)

children.title: (e) All Payment System Providers (PSPs) / System Participants
children.text: (e) All Payment System Providers (PSPs) / System Participants

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
(SPs) and Prepaid Payment Instrument Issuers (PPI Issuers)

children.title: (f) All authorised persons (APs), including those who are agents of
children.text:
(f) All authorised persons (APs), including those who are agents of
Money Transfer Service Scheme (MTSS), regulated by the
Regulator.

children.title: (xvi) 'Video based Customer Identification Process (V-CIP)': an
children.text:
(xvi) 'Video based Customer Identification Process (V-CIP)': an
alternative method by which an authorised official of the AIFI conducts
customer identification with facial recognition and customer due
diligence. This process involves a seamless, secure, live, informed-
consent based audio-visual interaction with the customer to obtain
identification information required for CDD purpose, and to ascertain
the veracity of the information which the customer furnished, through
independent verification and by maintaining an audit trail of the process.
The AIFI shall treat such processes complying with prescribed
standards and procedures on par with face-to-face CIP for the purpose
of this Direction.

children.title: (xvii) 'Wire transfer' related definitions:
children.text: (xvii) 'Wire transfer' related definitions:

children.title: (a) Batch transfer: A batch transfer is a transfer comprised of a
children.text:
(a) Batch transfer: A batch transfer is a transfer comprised of a
number of individual wire transfers that are being sent to the
same financial institutions but may / may not be ultimately
intended for different persons.

children.title: (b) Beneficiary: Beneficiary refers to a natural or legal person or
children.text:
(b) Beneficiary: Beneficiary refers to a natural or legal person or
legal arrangement whom / which the originator identifies as the
receiver of the requested wire transfer.

children.title: (c) Beneficiary RE: It refers to a financial institution that RBI
children.text:
(c) Beneficiary RE: It refers to a financial institution that RBI
regulates, which receives the wire transfer from the ordering
financial institution directly or through an intermediary RE and
makes the funds available to the beneficiary.

children.title: (d) Cover Payment: Cover Payment refers to a wire transfer that
children.text:
(d) Cover Payment: Cover Payment refers to a wire transfer that
combines a payment message which the ordering financial
institution sends directly to the beneficiary financial institution
with the routing of the funding instruction (the cover) from the
ordering financial institution to the beneficiary financial institution

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
through one or more intermediary financial institutions.

children.title: (e) Cross-border wire transfer: Cross-border wire transfer refers to
children.text:
(e) Cross-border wire transfer: Cross-border wire transfer refers to
any wire transfer where the ordering financial institution and
beneficiary financial institution are located in different countries.
This term also refers to any chain of wire transfer in which at
least one of the financial institutions involved is located in a
different country.

children.title: (f) Domestic wire transfer: Domestic wire transfer refers to any wire
children.text:
(f) Domestic wire transfer: Domestic wire transfer refers to any wire
transfer where the ordering financial institution and beneficiary
financial institution are located in India. This term, therefore,
refers to any chain of wire transfers that takes place entirely
within the borders of India, even though the system used to
transfer the payment message may be located in another
country.

children.title: (g) Financial Institution: In the context of wire-transfer instructions,
children.text:
(g) Financial Institution: In the context of wire-transfer instructions,
the term 'Financial Institution' shall have the same meaning as
has been ascribed to it in the FATF Recommendations, as
revised from time to time.

children.title: (h) Intermediary RE: Intermediary RE refers to an RBI regulated
children.text:
(h) Intermediary RE: Intermediary RE refers to an RBI regulated
financial institution / entity that handles an intermediary element
of the wire transfer, in a serial or cover payment chain and that
receives and transmits a wire transfer on behalf of the ordering
financial institution and the beneficiary financial institution, or
another intermediary financial institution.

children.title: (i) Ordering RE: Ordering RE refers to the RBI-regulated financial
children.text:
(i) Ordering RE: Ordering RE refers to the RBI-regulated financial
institution which initiates the wire transfer and transfers the funds
upon receiving the request for a wire transfer on behalf of the
originator.

children.title: (j) Originator: Originator refers to the account holder who allows the
children.text:
(j) Originator: Originator refers to the account holder who allows the
wire transfer from that account, or where there is no account, the
natural or legal person that places the order with the ordering
financial institution to perform the wire transfer.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (k) Serial Payment: Serial Payment refers to a direct sequential
children.text:
(k) Serial Payment: Serial Payment refers to a direct sequential
chain of payment where the wire transfer and accompanying
payment message travel together from the ordering financial
institution to the beneficiary financial institution directly or
through one or more intermediary financial institutions (e.g.,
correspondent banks).

children.title: (l) Straight-through Processing: Straight-through processing refers
children.text:
(l) Straight-through Processing: Straight-through processing refers
to payment transactions that are conducted electronically
without the need for manual intervention.

children.title: (m) Unique transaction reference number: Unique transaction
children.text:
(m) Unique transaction reference number: Unique transaction
reference number refers to a combination of letters, numbers or
symbols, a payment service provider determines, in accordance
with the protocols of the payment and settlement system or
messaging system used for the wire transfer.

children.title: (n) Wire transfer: Wire transfer refers to any transaction carried out
children.text:
(n) Wire transfer: Wire transfer refers to any transaction carried out
on behalf of an originator through a financial institution by
electronic means with a view to making an amount of funds
available to a beneficiary at a beneficiary financial institution,
irrespective of whether the originator and the beneficiary are the
same person.
(3) Unless defined herein, all other expressions shall have the same meaning
as has been assigned to them under the Banking Regulation Act, 1949,
the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1935, the Prevention of Money Laundering
Act, 2002, the Prevention of Money Laundering (Maintenance of Records)
Rules, 2005, the Aadhaar (Targeted Delivery of Financial and Other
Subsidies, Benefits and Services) Act, 2016 and regulations made
thereunder, any statutory modification or re-enactment thereto or as used
in commercial parlance, as the case may be.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: A. Board of Directors / Policies-related guidelines:
children.text:
A. Board of Directors / Policies-related guidelines:
6.
Know Your Customer (KYC) Policy:
(1) The AIFI shall have a KYC policy. The Board of Directors of the AIFI, or any
committee to which the power has been delegated shall duly approve the
KYC policy.
(2) The KYC policy shall include following four key elements:

children.title: (i) Customer Acceptance Policy;
children.text: (i) Customer Acceptance Policy;

children.title: (ii) Risk Management;
children.text: (ii) Risk Management;

children.title: (iii) Customer Identification Procedures (CIP); and
children.text: (iii) Customer Identification Procedures (CIP); and

children.title: (iv) Monitoring of Transactions
children.text:
(iv) Monitoring of Transactions
(3) The KYC policy shall, inter alia, incorporate provisions for the following:

children.title: (i) Periodic updation of KYC
children.text: (i) Periodic updation of KYC

children.title: (ii) Any exceptional measures for KYC updation, such as requiring a
children.text:
(ii) Any exceptional measures for KYC updation, such as requiring a
recent photograph, physical presence, or a more frequent updation

children.title: schedule than the minimum prescribed.
children.text: schedule than the minimum prescribed.

children.title: (iii) Obtaining a copy of OVD or deemed OVD, for the purpose of proof of
children.text:
(iii) Obtaining a copy of OVD or deemed OVD, for the purpose of proof of
change of address during KYC updation.

children.title: (iv) Providing facility of updation / periodic updation of KYC at any branch.
children.text:
(iv) Providing facility of updation / periodic updation of KYC at any branch.
(4) Furthermore, the AIFI shall have the policies approved by the Board
detailing a robust due diligence process for managing requests to change
the registered Mobile Number for Accounts opened in non-face-to-face
mode.
7.
The specific responsibilities and actions stipulated for the Board or its relevant
committees in paragraph 6 above are elaborated upon in greater detail within the
Directions.
8.
In terms of PML Rules, groups shall implement group-wide policies for the
purpose of discharging obligations under the provisions of Chapter IV of the PML
Act, 2002. (15 of 2003). Accordingly, every AIFI which is part of a group, shall

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
implement group-wide programmes against money laundering and terror
financing, including group-wide policies for sharing information required for the
purposes of client due diligence, money laundering, and terrorist finance risk
management, and such programmes shall include adequate safeguards on the
confidentiality and use of information exchanged, including safeguards to prevent
tipping-off.
9.
AIFI's policy framework shall seek to ensure compliance with PML Act / Rules,
including regulatory instructions in this regard and shall provide a bulwark against
threats arising from money laundering, terrorist financing, proliferation financing
and other related risks. While ensuring compliance with the legal / regulatory
requirements as above, the AIFI may also consider adoption of best international
practices taking into account the FATF standards and FATF guidance notes, for
managing risks better.

children.title: 10. Money Laundering and Terrorist Financing Risk Assessment by the AIFI:
children.text:
10. Money Laundering and Terrorist Financing Risk Assessment by the AIFI:
(1) The AIFI shall carry out 'Money Laundering (ML) and Terrorist Financing
(TF) Risk Assessment' exercises periodically to identify, assess and take
effective measures to mitigate its money laundering and terrorist financing
risk for clients, countries or geographic areas, products, services,
transactions or delivery channels, etc.
The assessment process shall consider all the relevant risk factors before
determining the level of overall risk and the appropriate level and type of
mitigation to be applied. While preparing the internal risk assessment, the
AIFI shall take cognizance of the overall sector-specific vulnerabilities, if
any, that the regulator / supervisor may share with the AIFI from time to
time.
(2) The AIFI shall properly document its risk assessment and it shall be
proportionate to the nature, size, geographical presence, complexity of
activities / structure, etc. of the AIFI. Further, the Board or a committee of
the Board to which it has delegated power shall determine the periodicity of
the risk assessment exercise, in alignment with the outcome of the risk
assessment exercise. However, the AIFI shall review it at least annually.
(3) The AIFI shall present the outcome of the exercise to the Board or any

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
committee of the Board to which power has been delegated in this regard.
The outcome shall also be made available to competent authorities and
self-regulating bodies.

children.title: 11. The AIFI shall apply a Risk Based Approach (RBA) for mitigation and
children.text:
11. The AIFI shall apply a Risk Based Approach (RBA) for mitigation and
management of the risks (identified on its own or through national risk
assessment) and shall have Board-approved policies, controls and procedures
in this regard. The AIFI shall implement a CDD programme, having regard to the
ML / TF risks identified and the size of business. Further, the AIFI shall monitor
the implementation of the controls and enhance them if necessary.

children.title: 12. Compliance of KYC policy: The AIFI shall ensure compliance with KYC Policy
children.text:
12. Compliance of KYC policy: The AIFI shall ensure compliance with KYC Policy
through:
(1) Specifying as to who constitute 'Senior Management' for the purpose of
KYC compliance.
(2) Allocation of responsibility for effective implementation of policies and
procedures.
(3) Independent evaluation of the compliance functions of the AIFI's policies
and procedures, including legal and regulatory requirements.
(4) Concurrent / internal audit system to verify compliance with KYC / AML
policies and procedures.
(5) Submission of quarterly audit notes and compliance to the Audit Committee.

children.title: 13. The AIFI shall ensure that it does not outsource the decision-making functions of
children.text:
13. The AIFI shall ensure that it does not outsource the decision-making functions of
determining compliance with KYC norms.

children.title: B. Other General Guidelines:
children.text: B. Other General Guidelines:

children.title: 14. Designated Director:
children.text:
14. Designated Director:
(1) A 'Designated Director' is a Board-nominated person whom the AIFI
designates to ensure overall compliance with the obligations imposed under

hierarchy.title: Chapter IV of the PML Act and the Rules.
hierarchy.text:
Chapter IV of the PML Act and the Rules.
(2) The AIFI shall communicate the name, designation, address, and contact
details of the Designated Director to the FIU-IND and RBI.
(3) The AIFI shall not nominate the Principal Officer as the 'Designated

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
Director'.

children.title: 15. Principal Officer:
children.text:
15. Principal Officer:
(1) The Principal Officer shall be responsible for ensuring compliance,
monitoring transactions, and sharing and reporting information as required
under the law / regulations.
(2) The AIFI shall communicate the name, designation, address, and contact
details of the Principal Officer to the FIU-IND and RBI.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: 16. The AIFI shall frame a Customer Acceptance Policy.
children.text: 16. The AIFI shall frame a Customer Acceptance Policy.

children.title: 17. Without prejudice to the generality of the aspect that Customer Acceptance Policy
children.text:
17. Without prejudice to the generality of the aspect that Customer Acceptance Policy
may contain, the AIFI shall:
(1) not open any account in an anonymous or fictitious / benami name.
(2) open no account where it is unable to apply appropriate CDD measures,
either due to non-cooperation of the customer or unreliability of the
documents / information furnished by the customer. The AIFI shall consider
filing an STR, if necessary, when it is unable to comply with the relevant
CDD measures in relation to the customer.
(3) not undertake a transaction or commence an account-based relationship
without following the CDD procedure.
(4) specify the mandatory information to be sought for KYC purposes while
opening an account and during the periodic updation.
(5) obtain additional information, where its internal KYC Policy has not
specified such information requirement, with the explicit consent of the
customer.
(6) apply the CDD procedure at the UCIC level. Thus, if an existing KYC-
compliant customer of a AIFI desires to open another account or avail of
any other product or service from the same AIFI, there shall be no need for
a fresh CDD exercise as far as identification of the customer is concerned.
(7) follow the CDD Procedure for all the joint account holders, while opening a
joint account
(8) clearly spell out the circumstances in which a customer is permitted to act
on behalf of another person / entity.
(9) put in place a suitable system to ensure that the identity of the customer
does not match with any person or entity, whose name appears in the
sanctions lists indicated in Chapter IX of these Directions.
(10) verify the Permanent Account Number (PAN) (if obtained) from the
verification facility of the issuing authority.

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
(11) verify the customer's digital signature on the equivalent e-document (if
obtained) as per the provisions of the Information Technology Act, 2000 (21
of 2000).
(12) verify the Goods and Services Tax (GST) number from the search /
verification facility of the issuing authority, where the GST details are
available.

children.title: 18. Customer Acceptance Policy shall not result in denial of a banking / financial
children.text:
18. Customer Acceptance Policy shall not result in denial of a banking / financial
facility to members of the general public, especially those who are financially or
socially disadvantaged, including the Persons with Disabilities (PwDs). The AIFI
shall not reject an application for onboarding or periodic updation of KYC without
application of mind. The officer concerned shall duly record the reason(s) for
rejection.

children.title: 19. Where the AIFI forms a suspicion of money laundering or terrorist financing, and
children.text:
19. Where the AIFI forms a suspicion of money laundering or terrorist financing, and
it reasonably believes that performing the CDD process will tip off the customer,
it shall not pursue the CDD process and instead file an STR with FIU-IND.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: 20. For risk management, the AIFI shall have a risk-based approach which includes
children.text:
20. For risk management, the AIFI shall have a risk-based approach which includes
the following.
(1) The AIFI shall categorise customers into low, medium, and high-risk
categories, based on its assessment and risk perception.
(2) The AIFI may lay down broad principles for the risk-categorisation of
customers.
(3) The AIFI shall undertake risk categorisation based on parameters such as
the customer's identity, social / financial status, nature of business activity,
and information about the customer's business and its location,
geographical risk covering customers as well as transactions, type of
products / services offered, delivery channel used for delivery of products /
services, types of transactions undertaken such as cash, cheque /
monetary instruments, wire transfers, forex transactions, etc. The AIFI may
also factor in the ability to confirm identity documents through online or
other services offered by issuing authorities, while considering customer's
identity.
(4) The AIFI shall keep the risk categorisation of a customer and the specific
reasons for such categorisation confidential and shall not reveal this
information to the customer to avoid tipping off.
Provided that the AIFI collects various other non-intrusive information from
different categories of customers relating to the perceived risk, and
specifies the same in the KYC policy.

children.title: Explanation: The AIFI may also use the FATF Public Statement, the reports
children.text:
Explanation: The AIFI may also use the FATF Public Statement, the reports
and guidance notes on KYC / AML issued by the Indian Banks Association
(IBA), and other agencies, etc., in its risk assessment.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: 21. The AIFI shall undertake identification of customers in the following cases:
children.text:
21. The AIFI shall undertake identification of customers in the following cases:
(1) Commencement of an account-based relationship with the customer.
(2) Carrying out any international money transfer operations for a person who is
not an account holder of the AIFI.
(3) When there is a doubt about the authenticity or adequacy of the customer
identification data it has obtained.
(4) When the AIFI has reason to believe that a customer (account-based or walk-
in) is intentionally structuring a transaction into a series of transactions
below the threshold of ₹50,000.
(5) The AIFI shall ensure it does not seek introductions while opening accounts.

children.title: 22. For the purpose of verifying the identity of customers at the time of
children.text:
22. For the purpose of verifying the identity of customers at the time of
commencement of an account-based relationship or while carrying out an
occasional transaction of an amount equal to or exceeding ₹50,000, whether
conducted as a single transaction or several transactions that appear to be
connected, or any international money transfer operations, the AIFI, shall at its
option, rely on customer due diligence done by a third party, subject to the
following conditions:
(1) The AIFI obtains the records or information of the customer due diligence
carried out by the third party immediately from the third party or from the
Central KYC Records Registry.
(2) The AIFI shall take adequate steps to satisfy itself that the third party will
make copies of identification data and other relevant documentation relating
to the customer due diligence requirements available, upon request, without
delay.
(3) A regulator regulates, supervises, or monitors the third party, and the third
party has measures in place for compliance with customer due diligence
and record-keeping requirements in line with the requirements and
obligations under the PML Act.
(4) The AIFI shall ensure that the third party is not based in a country or

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
jurisdiction assessed as high-risk.
(5) The AIFI will have the ultimate responsibility for customer due diligence and
undertaking enhanced due diligence measures, as applicable.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

hierarchy.title: Chapter VI - Customer Due Diligence (CDD) Procedure
hierarchy.text: Chapter VI - Customer Due Diligence (CDD) Procedure

children.title: A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals
children.text: A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals

children.title: 23. For undertaking CDD, the AIFI shall obtain the following from an individual while
children.text:
23. For undertaking CDD, the AIFI shall obtain the following from an individual while
establishing an account-based relationship or while dealing with the individual
who is a beneficial owner, authorised signatory or the power of attorney holder
related to any legal entity:
(1) the Aadhaar number where,

children.title: (i) they are desirous of receiving any benefit or subsidy under any
children.text:
(i) they are desirous of receiving any benefit or subsidy under any
scheme notified under section 7 of the Aadhaar (Targeted Delivery of
Financial and Other subsidies, Benefits and Services) Act, 2016 (18
of 2016); or

children.title: (ii) they decide to submit their Aadhaar number voluntarily to a AIFI or
children.text:
(ii) they decide to submit their Aadhaar number voluntarily to a AIFI or
any RE notified under the first proviso to sub-section (1) of section
11A of the PML Act; or
(2) the proof of possession of Aadhaar number where the AIFI can carry out
offline verification; or
(3) the proof of possession of Aadhaar number where the AIFI cannot carry out
the offline verification or any OVD or the equivalent e-document thereof
containing the details of their identity and address; or
(4) the KYC Identifier with an explicit consent to download records from
CKYCR; and
(5) the PAN or the equivalent e-document thereof or Form No. 60 as defined
in Income-tax Rules, 1962; and
(6) the AIFI may require such other documents including in respect of the
nature of business and financial status of the customer, or the equivalent e-
documents thereof.
Provided that where the customer has submitted,

children.title: (i) Aadhaar number under clause (1) above to a AIFI notified under first
children.text:
(i) Aadhaar number under clause (1) above to a AIFI notified under first
proviso to sub-section (1) of section 11A of the PML Act, such AIFI shall
carry out authentication of the customer's Aadhaar number using

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
UIDAI's e-KYC authentication facility. Further, in such a case, if the
customer wants to provide a current address, different from the address
as per the identity information available in the Central Identities Data
Repository, they may give a self-declaration to that effect to the AIFI.

children.title: (ii) proof of possession of Aadhaar under clause (2) above where offline
children.text:
(ii) proof of possession of Aadhaar under clause (2) above where offline
verification can be carried out, the AIFI shall carry out offline
verification.

children.title: (iii) an equivalent e-document of any OVD, the AIFI shall verify the digital
children.text:
(iii) an equivalent e-document of any OVD, the AIFI shall verify the digital
signature as per the provisions of the Information Technology Act, 2000
(21 of 2000) and any rules issued thereunder and take a live photo as
specified under paragraph 24 below.

children.title: (iv) any OVD or proof of possession of Aadhaar number under clause (3)
children.text:
(iv) any OVD or proof of possession of Aadhaar number under clause (3)
above where offline verification cannot be carried out, the AIFI shall
carry out verification through digital KYC as specified under paragraph
24 below.

children.title: (v) KYC Identifier under clause (4) above, the AIFI shall retrieve the
children.text:
(v) KYC Identifier under clause (4) above, the AIFI shall retrieve the
KYC records online from the CKYCR in accordance with paragraph 62.
Provided that for a period not beyond such date as the Government may
notify for a class of REs, instead of carrying out digital KYC, the AIFI
pertaining to such class may obtain a certified copy of the proof of
possession of Aadhaar number or the OVD and a recent photograph where
the customer does not submit an equivalent e- document.
Provided further that in case the AIFI cannot perform an e-KYC
authentication for an individual desirous of receiving any benefit or subsidy
under any scheme notified under section 7 of the Aadhaar (Targeted
Delivery of Financial and Other subsidies, Benefits and Services) Act, 2016
owing to injury, illness or infirmity on account of old age or otherwise, and
similar causes, the AIFI shall, apart from obtaining the Aadhaar number,
perform identification preferably by carrying out offline verification or
alternatively by obtaining the certified copy of any other OVD or the
equivalent e-document thereof from the customer. An official of the AIFI
shall invariably carry out CDD done in this manner, and such exception

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
handling shall also be a part of the concurrent audit as mandated in
paragraphs 12. The AIFI shall ensure to duly record the cases of exception
handling in a centralised exception database. The database shall contain
the details of grounds of granting exception, customer details, name of the
designated official authorising the exception and additional details, if any.
The AIFI shall subject the database to periodic internal audit / inspection and
the AIFI shall make database available for supervisory review.

children.title: Explanation 1: The AIFI shall, where its customer submits a proof of
children.text:
Explanation 1: The AIFI shall, where its customer submits a proof of
possession of Aadhaar Number containing Aadhaar Number, ensure that
such customer redacts or blacks out his Aadhaar number through
appropriate means where the authentication of Aadhaar number is not
required as per proviso (i) above.

children.title: Explanation 2: An AIFI official can perform biometric-based e-KYC
children.text:
Explanation 2: An AIFI official can perform biometric-based e-KYC
authentication, including Aadhaar Face Authentication.

children.title: Explanation 3: The AIFI shall ensure that the use of Aadhaar, proof of
children.text:
Explanation 3: The AIFI shall ensure that the use of Aadhaar, proof of
possession of Aadhaar etc., is in accordance with the Aadhaar (Targeted
Delivery of Financial and Other Subsidies Benefits and Services) Act, 2016
and the regulations made thereunder.

children.title: Explanation 4: Aadhaar number is not mandatory for purposes of KYC.
children.text:
Explanation 4: Aadhaar number is not mandatory for purposes of KYC.
However, in case the customer is desirous of receiving any benefit or
subsidy under any scheme notified under section 7 of the Aadhaar
(Targeted Delivery of Financial and Other subsidies, Benefits and Services)
Act, 2016 (18 of 2016), the customer shall provide the Aadhaar number to
the AIFI. In other cases, customers may provide the Aadhaar
number voluntarily.

children.title: 24. Digital KYC Process:
children.text:
24. Digital KYC Process:
(1) The AIFI shall develop an application for digital KYC process and make it
available at customer touch points for undertaking KYC of its customers
and shall undertake the KYC process only through this authenticated
application.
(2) The AIFI shall control the access to the Application and shall ensure that
unauthorised persons do not use it. Authorised officials shall access the

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
Application only through a login-id and password or a Live OTP or Time
OTP controlled mechanism that the AIFI provides.
(3) The customer, for the purpose of KYC, shall visit the location of the
authorised official of the AIFI or vice-versa. The original OVD shall be in
possession of the customer.
(4) The AIFI shall ensure that the authorised officer takes a Live photograph of
the customer and embeds the same photograph in the Customer
Application Form (CAF). Further, the AIFI's system Application shall put a
watermark in readable form, containing the CAF number, GPS coordinates,
authorised official's name, unique employee code (which the AIFI assigns)
and date (DD:MM:YYYY) and time stamp (HH:MM:SS), on the captured live
photograph of the customer.
(5) The AIFI's Application shall have the feature that it captures only a live
photograph of the customer and does not capture any printed or video
graphed photograph. The background behind the customer while capturing
live photograph shall be of white colour and no other person shall come into
the frame while capturing the live photograph of the customer.
(6) Similarly, the authorised officer shall capture the live photograph of the
original OVD or proof of possession of Aadhaar where offline verification
cannot be carried out (placed horizontally), vertically from above and shall
apply a water-marking in readable form as mentioned above. The
authorised officer shall ensure there is no skew or tilt in the mobile device
while capturing the live photograph of the original documents.
(7) The authorised officer shall capture the live photograph of the customer and
his original documents in proper light so that they are clearly readable and
identifiable.
(8) Thereafter, the authorised officer shall fill all the entries in the CAF as per
the documents and information furnished by the customer. In those
documents where Quick Response (QR) code is available, such details
may be auto-populated by scanning the QR code instead of manual filing
the details. For example, in case of physical Aadhaar / e-Aadhaar
downloaded from UIDAI where QR code is available, the details like name,

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
gender, date of birth and address may be auto-populated by scanning the
QR available on Aadhaar / e-Aadhaar.
(9) Once the above-mentioned process is completed, a One Time Password
(OTP) message containing the text that 'Please verify the details filled in
form before sharing OTP' shall be sent to customer's own mobile number.
Upon successful validation of the OTP, the AIFI will treat it as the
customer's signature on CAF. However, if the customer does not have their
own mobile number, the AIFI may use the mobile number of their family /
relatives / known persons for this purpose and clearly mention it in the CAF.
In any case, the AIFI shall not use the mobile number of authorised officer
registered with the AIFI for the customer signature. The AIFI shall check
that the mobile number used in customer signature is not the mobile
number of the authorised officer.
(10) The authorised officer shall provide a declaration about the capturing of the
live photograph of the customer and original document. For this purpose,
the AIFI shall verify the authorised officer with One Time Password (OTP)
which will be sent to his mobile number registered with the AIFI. Upon
successful OTP validation, the AIFI shall treat it as the authorised officer's
signature on the declaration. The live photograph of the authorised officer
shall also be captured in this authorised officer's declaration.
(11) Subsequent to all these activities, the Application shall give information
about the completion of the process and submission of activation request
to activation officer of the AIFI, and also generate the transaction-id /
reference-id number of the process. The authorised officer shall intimate
the details regarding transaction-id / reference-id number to the customer
for future reference.
(12) The authorised officer of the AIFI shall check and verify that:

children.title: (i) information available in the picture of the document matches with the
children.text:
(i) information available in the picture of the document matches with the
information entered by authorised officer in CAF;

children.title: (ii) live photograph of the customer matches with the photo available in the
children.text:
(ii) live photograph of the customer matches with the photo available in the
document; and

children.title: (iii) the authorised officer has properly filled all of the necessary details in
children.text: (iii) the authorised officer has properly filled all of the necessary details in

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
CAF, including mandatory field.
(13) On Successful verification, the CAF shall be digitally signed by authorised
officer of the AIFI who will take a print of CAF, get signatures / thumb-
impression of customer at appropriate place, then scan and upload the
same in system. Original hard copy may be returned to the customer.

children.title: 25. Accounts opened using Aadhaar OTP based e-KYC, in non-face-to-face mode,
children.text:
25. Accounts opened using Aadhaar OTP based e-KYC, in non-face-to-face mode,
are subject to the following conditions:
(1) The Customer shall give specific consent for the authentication through
OTP.
(2) As a risk-mitigating measure for such accounts, the AIFI shall ensure that
it sends transaction alerts, OTP, etc., only to the mobile number of the
customer registered with Aadhaar. The AIFI shall have a Board-approved
policy delineating a robust process of due diligence for dealing with
requests for change of mobile number in such accounts.
(3) The aggregate balance of all the deposit accounts of the customer shall not
exceed Rupees One Lakh. In case the balance exceeds the threshold, the
AIFI shall cease the account's operation, until it completes the CDD as
mentioned at (6) below.
(4) The aggregate of all credits in a financial year, in all the deposit accounts
taken together, shall not exceed Rupees Two Lakh.
(5) As regards borrowal accounts, the AIFI shall sanction only term loans. The
aggregate amount of term loans sanctioned shall not exceed ₹60,000 in a
year.
(6) The AIFI shall not allow accounts, both deposit and borrowal, opened using
OTP based e-KYC to operate for more than one year unless it carries out
identification as per paragraph 23 or as per paragraphs 26 and 27 (V-CIP).
If the AIFI uses Aadhaar details under paragraphs 26 and 27, it shall follow
the process in its entirety, including fresh Aadhaar OTP authentication.
(7) If the AIFI does not complete the CDD procedure as mentioned above within
a year; (a) in respect of deposit accounts, the AIFI shall close the same
immediately, and (b) in respect of borrowal accounts, the AIFI shall allow no

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
further debits.
(8) The AIFI shall obtain declaration from the customer to the effect that no other
account has been opened nor will be opened using OTP based KYC in non-
face-to-face mode with any other RE. Further, while uploading KYC
information to CKYCR, AIFI shall clearly indicate that such accounts are
opened using OTP based e-KYC and other REs shall not open accounts
based on the KYC information of accounts opened with OTP based e-KYC
procedure in non-face- to-face mode.
(9) The AIFI shall have strict monitoring procedures including systems to
generate alerts in case of any non-compliance / violation, to ensure
compliance with the above-mentioned conditions.

children.title: 26. The AIFI may undertake V-CIP to carry out:
children.text:
26. The AIFI may undertake V-CIP to carry out:
(1) CDD in case of new customer onboarding for individual customers,
proprietor in case of proprietorship firm, authorised signatories and
Beneficial Owners (BOs) in case of Legal Entity (LE) customers.
Provided that in case of CDD of a proprietorship firm, the AIFI shall also
obtain the equivalent e-document of the activity proofs with respect to the
proprietorship firm, as mentioned in paragraph 30 and paragraph 31, apart
from undertaking CDD of the proprietor.
(2) Conversion of existing accounts opened in non-face-to-face mode using
Aadhaar OTP based e-KYC authentication as per paragraph 25.
(3) Updation / Periodic updation of KYC for eligible customers.

children.title: 27. The AIFI opting to undertake V-CIP, shall adhere to the following minimum
children.text:
27. The AIFI opting to undertake V-CIP, shall adhere to the following minimum
standards:
(1) V-CIP Infrastructure

children.title: (i) The AIFI shall have complied with the RBI guidelines on minimum
children.text:
(i) The AIFI shall have complied with the RBI guidelines on minimum
baseline cyber security and resilience framework for AIFIs, as updated
from time to time as well as other general guidelines on IT risks. The
AIFI shall house the technology infrastructure on its own premises and
the V-CIP connection and interaction shall necessarily originate from its
own secured network domain. Any technology related outsourcing for

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
the process shall comply with relevant RBI guidelines. Where the AIFI
uses a cloud deployment model, it shall ensure that ownership of data
in such model rests with the AIFI only and all the data including video
recording is transferred to the AIFI's exclusively owned / leased
server(s) including cloud server, if any, immediately after the V-CIP
process is completed and the cloud service provider or third-party
technology provider assisting the V-CIP shall retain no data.

children.title: (ii) The AIFI shall ensure end-to-end encryption of data between customer
children.text:
(ii) The AIFI shall ensure end-to-end encryption of data between customer
device and the hosting point of the V-CIP application, as per
appropriate encryption standards. The AIFI shall record the customer
consent in an auditable and alteration-proof manner.

children.title: (iii) The V-CIP infrastructure / application shall be capable of preventing
children.text:
(iii) The V-CIP infrastructure / application shall be capable of preventing
connection from IP addresses outside India or from spoofed IP
addresses.

children.title: (iv) The video recordings shall contain the live GPS co-ordinates (geo-
children.text:
(iv) The video recordings shall contain the live GPS co-ordinates (geo-
tagging) of the customer undertaking the V-CIP and date and time
stamp. The quality of the live video in the V-CIP shall be adequate to
allow identification of the customer beyond doubt.

children.title: (v) The application shall have components with face liveness / spoof
children.text:
(v) The application shall have components with face liveness / spoof
detection as well as face matching technology with high degree of
accuracy, even though the ultimate responsibility of any customer
identification rests with the AIFI.

children.title: Explanation: Making specific facial gestures, like blinking of eyes,
children.text:
Explanation: Making specific facial gestures, like blinking of eyes,
smiling, frowning, etc. is not mandatory for liveness check. The AIFI
shall take due cognizance of special needs, if any, of the customer
during liveness check.

children.title: (vi) The AIFI may use appropriate artificial intelligence (AI) technology to
children.text:
(vi) The AIFI may use appropriate artificial intelligence (AI) technology to
ensure that the V-CIP is robust.

children.title: (vii) Based on experience of detected / attempted / 'near-miss' cases of
children.text:
(vii) Based on experience of detected / attempted / 'near-miss' cases of
forged identity, the AIFI shall regularly update the technology
infrastructure including application software as well as workflows. The
AIFI shall report any detected case of forged identity through V-CIP as

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
a cyber event under extant regulatory guidelines.

children.title: (viii) The AIFI shall subject the V-CIP infrastructure to necessary tests such
children.text:
(viii) The AIFI shall subject the V-CIP infrastructure to necessary tests such
as Vulnerability Assessment, Penetration testing and a Security Audit
to ensure its robustness and end-to-end encryption capabilities. The
AIFI shall mitigate any critical gap reported under this process before
rolling out its implementation. The empanelled auditors of Indian
Computer Emergency Response Team (CERT-In) shall conduct such
tests. Such tests shall also be carried out periodically in conformance
to internal / regulatory guidelines.

children.title: (ix) The AIFI shall subject the V-CIP application software and relevant APIs
children.text:
(ix) The AIFI shall subject the V-CIP application software and relevant APIs
/ webservices to appropriate testing of functional, performance, and
maintenance strength before being used in live environment. The AIFI
shall roll out the application only after closure of any critical gap found
during such tests. Such tests shall also be carried out periodically in
conformity with internal / regulatory guidelines.
(2) V-CIP Procedure

children.title: (i) Each AIFI shall formulate a clear workflow and standard operating
children.text:
(i) Each AIFI shall formulate a clear workflow and standard operating
procedure for V-CIP and ensure adherence to it. The V-CIP process
shall be operated only by officials of the AIFI specially trained for this
purpose. The official shall be capable to carry out liveness check and
detect any other fraudulent manipulation or suspicious conduct of the
customer and act upon it. The liveness check shall not result in
exclusion of person with special needs.

children.title: (ii) Disruption of any sort including pausing of video, reconnecting calls,
children.text:
(ii) Disruption of any sort including pausing of video, reconnecting calls,
etc., may not result in creation of multiple video files. If pause or
disruption is not leading to the creation of multiple files, then the AIFI
may not initiate a fresh session. However, in case of call drop /
disconnection, fresh session shall be initiated.

children.title: (iii) The AIFI shall vary the sequence and / or type of questions, including
children.text:
(iii) The AIFI shall vary the sequence and / or type of questions, including
those indicating the liveness of the interaction, during video interactions
to establish that the interactions are real-time and not pre-recorded.

children.title: (iv) The AIFI shall reject the account opening process if it observes any
children.text: (iv) The AIFI shall reject the account opening process if it observes any

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
prompting at the customer end.

children.title: (v) The AIFI shall factor in the fact that the V-CIP customer is an existing
children.text:
(v) The AIFI shall factor in the fact that the V-CIP customer is an existing
or new customer, or if the case relates to one rejected earlier or if the
name appears in some negative list, at an appropriate stage of
workflow.

children.title: (vi) The authorised official of the AIFI performing the V-CIP shall record
children.text:
(vi) The authorised official of the AIFI performing the V-CIP shall record
audio and video as well as capture a photograph of the customer
present for identification and obtain the identification information using
any one of the following:

children.title: (a) OTP based Aadhaar e-KYC authentication.
children.text: (a) OTP based Aadhaar e-KYC authentication.

children.title: (b) Offline Verification of Aadhaar for identification.
children.text: (b) Offline Verification of Aadhaar for identification.

children.title: (c) KYC records downloaded from CKYCR, in accordance with
children.text:
(c) KYC records downloaded from CKYCR, in accordance with
paragraph 62, using the KYC identifier provided by the customer.

children.title: (d) Equivalent e-document of Officially Valid Documents (OVDs)
children.text:
(d) Equivalent e-document of Officially Valid Documents (OVDs)
including documents issued through DigiLocker.

children.title: (vii) The AIFI shall ensure to redact or blackout the Aadhaar number in
children.text:
(vii) The AIFI shall ensure to redact or blackout the Aadhaar number in
terms of paragraph 23.

children.title: (viii) In case of offline verification of Aadhaar using XML file or Aadhaar
children.text:
(viii) In case of offline verification of Aadhaar using XML file or Aadhaar
Secure QR Code, the AIFI shall ensure that the XML file or QR code
generation date is not older than three working days from the date of
carrying out V-CIP.

children.title: (ix) Further, in line with the prescribed period of three working days for
children.text:
(ix) Further, in line with the prescribed period of three working days for
usage of Aadhaar XML file / Aadhaar QR code, the AIFI shall ensure
that it undertakes video process of the V-CIP within three working days
of downloading / obtaining the identification information through
CKYCR / Aadhaar authentication / equivalent e-document, if in the rare
cases, the entire process cannot be completed at one go or seamlessly.
However, the AIFI shall ensure that no incremental risk is added due to
this.

children.title: (x) If the address of the customer is different from that indicated in the
children.text:
(x) If the address of the customer is different from that indicated in the
OVD, the AIFI shall capture suitable records of the current address, as

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
per the existing requirement. The AIFI shall ensure that it also confirms
the economic and financial profile / information submitted by the
customer from the customer undertaking the V-CIP in a suitable
manner.

children.title: (xi) The AIFI shall capture a clear image of PAN card displayed by the
children.text:
(xi) The AIFI shall capture a clear image of PAN card displayed by the
customer during the process, except in cases where e-PAN is provided
by the customer. The AIFI shall verify the PAN details from the
database of the issuing authority, including through DigiLocker.

children.title: (xii) The use of printed copy of equivalent e-document, including an e-PAN
children.text:
(xii) The use of printed copy of equivalent e-document, including an e-PAN
is not valid for the V-CIP.

children.title: (xiii) The authorised official of the AIFI shall ensure that photograph of the
children.text:
(xiii) The authorised official of the AIFI shall ensure that photograph of the
customer in the Aadhaar / OVD and PAN / e-PAN matches with the
customer undertaking the V-CIP and the identification details in
Aadhaar / OVD and PAN / e-PAN shall match with the details provided
by the customer.

children.title: (xiv) The AIFI shall make all accounts opened through V-CIP operational
children.text:
(xiv) The AIFI shall make all accounts opened through V-CIP operational
only after subjecting them to concurrent audit to ensure the integrity of
process and its acceptability of its outcome.

children.title: (xv) The AIFI shall appropriately comply with all matters not specified under
children.text:
(xv) The AIFI shall appropriately comply with all matters not specified under
the paragraph but required under other statutes such as the Information
Technology (IT) Act.
(3) V-CIP Records and Data Management

children.title: (i) The AIFI shall store the entire data and recordings of V-CIP in a system
children.text:
(i) The AIFI shall store the entire data and recordings of V-CIP in a system
/ system located in India. The AIFI shall ensure that the video
recording is stored in a safe and secure manner and bears the date
and time stamp that affords easy historical data search. The extant
instructions on record management, as stipulated in this direction,
shall also apply to V-CIP.

children.title: (ii) The AIFI shall preserve the activity log along with the credentials of
children.text:
(ii) The AIFI shall preserve the activity log along with the credentials of
the official performing the V-CIP.

children.title: 28. KYC verification once done by one branch / office of the AIFI shall be valid for
children.text: 28. KYC verification once done by one branch / office of the AIFI shall be valid for

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
transfer of the account to any other branch / office of the same AIFI, provided the
AIFI has already completed the full KYC verification for the concerned account
and the same is not due for periodic updation.

children.title: B. CDD Measures for Sole Proprietary firms
children.text: B. CDD Measures for Sole Proprietary firms

children.title: 29. For opening an account in the name of a sole proprietary firm, the AIFI shall carry
children.text:
29. For opening an account in the name of a sole proprietary firm, the AIFI shall carry
out the CDD of the individual (proprietor).

children.title: 30. In addition to the above, the AIFI shall also obtain any two of the following
children.text:
30. In addition to the above, the AIFI shall also obtain any two of the following
documents or the equivalent e-documents thereof as proof of business / activity
in the name of the proprietary firm:
(1) Registration certificate including Udyam Registration Certificate (URC)
issued by the Government.
(2) Certificate / licence issued by the municipal authorities under Shop and
Establishment Act
(3) Sales and income tax returns
(4) CST / VAT / GST certificate
(5) Certificate / registration document issued by Sales Tax / Service Tax /
Professional Tax authorities
(6) IEC (Importer Exporter Code) issued to the proprietary concern by the office
of DGFT or Licence / certificate of practice issued in the name of the
proprietary concern by any professional body incorporated under a statute
(7) Complete Income Tax Return (not just the acknowledgement) in the name
