of the sole proprietor where the firm's income is reflected, duly
authenticated / acknowledged by the Income Tax authorities
(8) Utility bills such as electricity, water, landline telephone bills, etc.

children.title: 31. In cases where the AIFI is satisfied that it is not possible to furnish two such
children.text:
31. In cases where the AIFI is satisfied that it is not possible to furnish two such
documents, the AIFI may, at its discretion, accept only one of those documents
as proof of business / activity.
Provided that the AIFI undertakes contact point verification and collects such
other information and clarifications as would be required to establish the existence
of such firm, and shall confirm and satisfy itself that it has verified the business

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
activity from the address of the proprietary concern.

children.title: C. CDD Measures for Legal Entities
children.text: C. CDD Measures for Legal Entities

children.title: 32. For opening an account of a company, the AIFI shall obtain certified copies of
children.text:
32. For opening an account of a company, the AIFI shall obtain certified copies of
each of the following documents or the equivalent e-documents thereof:
(1) Certificate of incorporation
(2) Memorandum and Articles of Association
(3) PAN of the company
(4) A resolution from the Board of Directors and power of attorney granted to
its managers, officers or employees to transact on its behalf
(5) Documents, as specified in paragraph 23, relating to beneficial owner,
managers, officers or employees, as the case may be, holding an attorney
to transact on the company's behalf
(6) The names of the relevant persons holding a senior management position;
and
(7) The registered office and the principal place of its business, if it is different.

children.title: 33. For opening an account of a partnership firm, the AIFI shall obtain the certified
children.text:
33. For opening an account of a partnership firm, the AIFI shall obtain the certified
copies of each of the following documents or the equivalent e-documents thereof:
(1) Registration certificate
(2) Partnership deed
(3) PAN of the partnership firm
(4) Documents, as specified in paragraph 23, relating to beneficial owner,
managers, officers or employees, as the case may be, holding an attorney
to transact on its behalf
(5) the names of all the partners and
(6) address of the registered office, and the principal place of its business, if it
is different.

children.title: 34. For opening an account of a trust, certified copies of each of the following
children.text:
34. For opening an account of a trust, certified copies of each of the following
documents or the equivalent e-documents thereof shall be obtained:

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
(1) Registration certificate
(2) Trust deed
(3) PAN or Form No.60 of the trust
(4) Documents, as specified in paragraph 16, relating to beneficial owner,
managers, officers or employees, as the case may be, holding an attorney
to transact on its behalf
(5) the names of the beneficiaries, trustees, settlor, protector, if any and
authors of the trust
(6) the address of the registered office of the trust; and
(7) list of trustees and documents, as specified in paragraph 16, for those
discharging the role as trustee and authorised to transact on behalf of the
trust.

children.title: 35. For opening an account of an unincorporated association or a body of individuals,
children.text:
35. For opening an account of an unincorporated association or a body of individuals,
the AIFI shall obtain the certified copies of each of the following documents or the
equivalent e-documents thereof:
(1) Resolution of the managing body of such association or body of individuals
(2) PAN or Form No. 60 of the unincorporated association or a body of
individuals
(3) Power of attorney granted to transact on its behalf
(4) Documents, as specified in paragraph 23, relating to beneficial owner,
managers, officers or employees, as the case may be, holding an attorney
to transact on its behalf and
(5) Such information as may be required by the AIFI to collectively establish
the legal existence of such an association or body of individuals.

children.title: Explanation: Unregistered trusts / partnership firms shall be included under
children.text:
Explanation: Unregistered trusts / partnership firms shall be included under
the term 'unincorporated association'.

children.title: Explanation: The term 'body of individuals' includes societies.
children.text: Explanation: The term 'body of individuals' includes societies.

children.title: 36. For opening account of a customer who is a juridical person (not specifically
children.text:
36. For opening account of a customer who is a juridical person (not specifically
covered in the earlier part) such as societies, universities and local bodies like

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
village panchayats, etc., or who purports to act on behalf of such juridical person
or individual or trust, the AIFI shall obtain and verify the certified copies of the
following documents or the equivalent e-documents thereof:
(1) Document showing name of the person authorised to act on behalf of the
entity
(2) Documents, as specified in paragraph 23, of the person holding an attorney
to transact on its behalf and
(3) Such documents as may be required by the AIFI to establish the legal
existence of such an entity / juridical person.
Provided that in case of a trust, the AIFI shall ensure that trustees disclose
their status at the time of commencement of an account-based relationship
or when carrying out transactions as specified in clause (2) and (5) of
paragraph 21 of these directions.

children.title: D. Identification of Beneficial Owner
children.text: D. Identification of Beneficial Owner

children.title: 37. For opening an account of a Legal Person who is not a natural person, the AIFI
children.text:
37. For opening an account of a Legal Person who is not a natural person, the AIFI
shall identify the beneficial owner(s) and shall undertake all reasonable steps in
terms of sub- rule (3) of Rule 9 of the Rules to verify their identity, keeping in view
the following:
(1) Where the customer or the owner of the controlling interest is:

children.title: (i) an entity listed on a stock exchange in India, or
children.text: (i) an entity listed on a stock exchange in India, or

children.title: (ii) it is an entity resident in jurisdictions notified by the Central
children.text:
(ii) it is an entity resident in jurisdictions notified by the Central
Government and listed on stock exchanges in such jurisdictions, or

children.title: (iii) it is a subsidiary of such listed entities; it is not necessary to identify
children.text:
(iii) it is a subsidiary of such listed entities; it is not necessary to identify
and verify the identity of any shareholder or beneficial owner of such
entities.
(2) In cases of trust / nominee or fiduciary accounts, the AIFI determines
whether the customer is acting on behalf of another person as trustee /
nominee or any other intermediary. In such cases, the AIFI shall obtain
satisfactory evidence of the identity of the intermediaries and of the persons
on whose behalf they are acting, as well as details of the nature of the trust

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
or other arrangements in place.

children.title: E. On-going Due Diligence
children.text: E. On-going Due Diligence

children.title: 38. The AIFI shall undertake ongoing due diligence of customers to ensure that their
children.text:
38. The AIFI shall undertake ongoing due diligence of customers to ensure that their
transactions are consistent with their knowledge about the customers, customers'
business and risk profile, the source of funds / wealth.

children.title: 39. Without prejudice to the generality of factors that call for close monitoring, the AIFI
children.text:
39. Without prejudice to the generality of factors that call for close monitoring, the AIFI
shall necessarily monitor the following types of transactions:
(1) Large and complex transactions including RTGS transactions, and those
with unusual patterns, inconsistent with the normal and expected activity of
the customer, which have no apparent economic rationale or legitimate
purpose.
(2) Transactions which exceed the thresholds prescribed for specific
categories of accounts.
(3) High account turnover inconsistent with the size of the balance maintained.
(4) Deposit of third-party cheques, drafts, etc. in the existing and newly opened
accounts followed by cash withdrawals for large amounts.
For ongoing due diligence, the AIFI may consider adopting appropriate
innovations including artificial intelligence and machine learning (AI and ML)
technologies to support effective monitoring.

children.title: 40. The AIFI shall align the extent of monitoring with the risk category of the
children.text:
40. The AIFI shall align the extent of monitoring with the risk category of the
customer.
(1) The AIFI shall put in place a system of periodic review of risk
categorisation of accounts, with such periodicity being at least once in every
six months, and shall establish the need for applying enhanced due
diligence measures.
(2) The AIFI shall closely monitor the transactions in accounts of marketing
firms, especially accounts of Multi-level Marketing (MLM) companies.

children.title: Explanation: The AIFI shall subject high-risk accounts to more intensified
children.text:
Explanation: The AIFI shall subject high-risk accounts to more intensified
monitoring.

children.title: Explanation: Cases where a large number of cheque books are sought
children.text: Explanation: Cases where a large number of cheque books are sought

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
by the company and/or multiple small deposits (generally in cash) across
the country in one AIFI account and / or where a large number of
cheques are issued bearing similar amounts / dates, shall be
immediately reported to RBI and other appropriate authorities such as
FIU-IND.

children.title: 41. Updation / Periodic Updation of KYC
children.text:
41. Updation / Periodic Updation of KYC
(1) The AIFI shall adopt a risk-based approach for periodic updation of KYC
ensuring that it keeps the information or data collected under CDD is kept
up-to-date and relevant, particularly where there is high risk. However, the
AIFI shall carry out periodic updation at least once in every two years for
high-risk customers, once in every eight years for medium risk customers
and once in every 10 years for low-risk customers from the date of opening
of the account / last KYC updation. The AIFI shall document its policy in this
regard as part of the AIFI's internal KYC policy duly approved by the
Board of Directors of AIFI or any committee of the Board to which power has
been delegated.
(2) Notwithstanding the provisions given above, in respect of an individual
customer who is categorised as low-risk, the AIFI shall allow all transactions
and ensure the updation of KYC within one year of its falling due for KYC
or up to June 30, 2026, whichever is later. The AIFI shall subject accounts
of such customers to regular monitoring. This shall also apply to low-risk
individual customers for whom periodic updation of KYC has already fallen
due.
(3) Individuals:

children.title: (i) No change in KYC information: In case of no change in the KYC
children.text:
(i) No change in KYC information: In case of no change in the KYC
information, the AIFI shall obtain a self-declaration from the customer
in this regard through the customer's email-id registered with the AIFI,
customer's mobile number registered with the AIFI or digital channels
(such as mobile application of AIFI) letter, etc.

children.title: (ii) Change in address: In case of a change only in the address details
children.text:
(ii) Change in address: In case of a change only in the address details
of the customer, the AIFI shall obtain a self-declaration of the new
address from the customer through customer's email-id registered

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
with the AIFI, customer's mobile number registered with the AIFI,
ATMs, digital channels (mobile application of AIFI), letter, etc., and
shall verify the declared address through positive confirmation within
two months, by means such as address verification letter, contact
point verification, deliverables, etc.

children.title: (iii) Accounts of customers, who were minor at the time of opening
children.text:
(iii) Accounts of customers, who were minor at the time of opening
account, on their becoming major:
In case of customers for whom account was opened when they were
minor, fresh photographs shall be obtained on their becoming a major
and at that time it shall be ensured that CDD documents as per the
current CDD standards are available with the REs. Wherever
required, REs may carry out fresh KYC of such customers i.e.,
customers for whom account was opened when they were minor, on
their becoming a major.

children.title: (iv) Further, the AIFI at its option, may obtain a copy of OVD or deemed
children.text:
(iv) Further, the AIFI at its option, may obtain a copy of OVD or deemed
OVD, as defined in sub-clause (xiv) of clause (1) of paragraph, or the
equivalent e-documents thereof, as defined in sub-clause (x) of clause
(1) of paragraph 5, for the purpose of proof of address, declared by
the customer at the time of updation / periodic updation. However, the
AIFI shall clearly specify such requirement, in its internal KYC policy
duly approved by the Board of Directors of the AIFI or any committee
of the Board to which power has been delegated.

children.title: (v) The AIFI may use Aadhaar OTP based e-KYC in non-face-to-face
children.text:
(v) The AIFI may use Aadhaar OTP based e-KYC in non-face-to-face
mode for updation / periodic updation. To clarify, conditions
stipulated in paragraph 25 are not applicable in case of updation /
periodic updation of KYC through Aadhaar OTP based e-KYC in non-
face to face mode.

children.title: (vi) Declaration of current address, if the current address is different from
children.text:
(vi) Declaration of current address, if the current address is different from
the address in Aadhaar, shall not require positive confirmation in this
case. The AIFI shall ensure that the mobile number for Aadhaar
authentication is same as the one available with them in the
customer's profile, in order to prevent any fraud.

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
(4) Customers other than individuals:

children.title: (i) No change in KYC information: In case of no change in the KYC
children.text:
(i) No change in KYC information: In case of no change in the KYC
information of the LE customer, the AIFI shall obtain a self-declaration
in this regard from the LE customer through its email id registered with
the AIFI, digital channels (such as mobile application of AIFI), letter
from an official authorised by the LE in this regard, board resolution,
etc. Further, the AIFI shall ensure during this process that Beneficial
Ownership (BO) information available with them is accurate and shall
update the same, if required, to keep it as up-to-date as possible.

children.title: (ii) Change in KYC information: In case of change in KYC information,
children.text:
(ii) Change in KYC information: In case of change in KYC information,
the AIFI shall undertake the KYC process equivalent to that applicable
for onboarding a new LE customer.
(5) Additional measures: In addition to the above, the AIFI shall ensure that:

children.title: (i) The AIFI has customer's KYC document as per the current CDD
children.text:
(i) The AIFI has customer's KYC document as per the current CDD
standards available with it. This is applicable even if there is no
change in customer information but the documents available with the
AIFI are not as per the current CDD standards. Further, in case the
validity of the CDD documents available with the AIFI has expired at
the time of periodic updation of KYC, the AIFI shall undertake the KYC
process equivalent to that applicable for on boarding a new customer.

children.title: (ii) The AIFI verifies the Customer's PAN details, if available, from the
children.text:
(ii) The AIFI verifies the Customer's PAN details, if available, from the
database of the issuing authority at the time of periodic updation of
KYC.

children.title: (iii) The AIFI provides an acknowledgment to the customer mentioning the
children.text:
(iii) The AIFI provides an acknowledgment to the customer mentioning the
date of receipt of the relevant document(s), including self-declaration
from the customer, for carrying out updation / periodic updation.
Further, the AIFI shall ensure that it promptly updates the information
/ documents obtained from the customers at the time of updation /
periodic updation of KYC in its records / database and provide an
intimation, mentioning the date of updation of KYC details, to the
customer.

children.title: (iv) In order to ensure customer convenience, the AIFI may consider
children.text: (iv) In order to ensure customer convenience, the AIFI may consider

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
making available the facility of updation / periodic updation of KYC at
any branch, in terms of their internal KYC policy duly approved by the
Board of Directors of the AIFI or any committee of the Board to which
power has been delegated.

children.title: (v) The AIFI shall adopt a risk-based approach with respect to periodic
children.text:
(v) The AIFI shall adopt a risk-based approach with respect to periodic
updation of KYC. The AIFI shall clearly specify in its internal policy,
duly approved by the Board of Directors of the AIFI or any committee
of the Board to which power has been delegated, any additional and
exceptional measures, it adopts that are not otherwise mandated
under the above instructions, such as requirement of obtaining recent
photograph, requirement of physical presence of the customer,
requirement of periodic updation of KYC only in the branch of the AIFI
where account is maintained, a more frequent periodicity of KYC
updation than the minimum specified periodicity etc.
(6) The AIFI shall advise the customers that in order to comply with the PML Rules,
in case of any update in the documents submitted by the customer at the time
of establishment of business relationship / account-based relationship and
thereafter, as necessary; customers shall submit to the AIFI the update of such
documents. This shall be done within 30 days of the update to the documents
for the purpose of updating the records at the AIFI's end.
(7) Due Notices for Periodic Updation of KYC: The AIFI shall intimate its
customers, in advance, to update their KYC. Prior to the due date of periodic
updation of KYC, the AIFI shall give at least three advance intimations, including
at least one intimation by letter, at appropriate intervals to its customers through
available communication options / channels for complying with the requirement
of periodic updation of KYC. Subsequent to the due date, the AIFI shall give at
least three reminders, including at least one reminder by letter, at appropriate
intervals, to such customers who have still not complied with the requirements,
despite advance intimations. The letter of intimation / reminder may, inter alia,
contain easy-to-understand instructions for updating KYC, escalation
mechanism for seeking help, if required, and the consequences, if any, of failure
to update their KYC in time. Issue of such advance intimation / reminder shall
be duly recorded in the AIFI's system against each customer for audit trail. The

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
AIFI shall expeditiously implement the same but not later than January 01,
2026.

children.title: 42. In case of existing customers, the AIFI shall obtain the PAN or equivalent e-
children.text:
42. In case of existing customers, the AIFI shall obtain the PAN or equivalent e-
document thereof or Form No. 60, by such date which the Central Government
may notify, failing which the AIFI shall temporarily cease operations in the
account until the customer submits the PAN or equivalent e-documents thereof
or Form No. 60.
Provided that before temporarily ceasing operations for an account, the AIFI shall
give the customer an accessible notice and a reasonable opportunity to be heard.
Further, the AIFI shall include, in its internal policy, appropriate relaxation(s) for
continued operation of accounts for customers who are unable to provide PAN
or equivalent e-document thereof or Form No. 60 owing to injury, illness or
infirmity on account of old age or otherwise, and such like causes. However, the
AIFI shall subject such accounts to enhanced monitoring.
Provided further that if a customer having an existing account-based relationship
with a AIFI gives in writing to the AIFI that they do not want to submit their PAN
or equivalent e-document thereof or Form No. 60, the AIFI shall close the account
and all obligations due in relation to the account shall be appropriately settled
after establishing the identity of the customer by obtaining the identification
documents as applicable to the customer.

children.title: Explanation: For the purpose of this paragraph, 'temporary ceasing of operations'
children.text:
Explanation: For the purpose of this paragraph, 'temporary ceasing of operations'
in relation to an account shall mean the temporary suspension of all transactions
or activities in relation to that account by the AIFI till such time the customer
complies with the provisions of this paragraph. In case of asset accounts such
as loan accounts, for the purpose of ceasing the operation in the account, only
credits shall be allowed.

children.title: F. Enhanced and Simplified Due Diligence Procedure
children.text:
F. Enhanced and Simplified Due Diligence Procedure
F1. Enhanced Due Diligence

children.title: 43. Enhanced Due Diligence (EDD) for non-face-to-face customer onboarding (other
children.text:
43. Enhanced Due Diligence (EDD) for non-face-to-face customer onboarding (other
than customer onboarding in terms of paragraph 25): Non-face-to-face
onboarding facilitates the AIFI to establish a relationship with the customer
without meeting the customer physically or through V-CIP. Such non-face-to-face

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
modes for the purpose of this paragraph include use of digital channels such as
CKYCR, DigiLocker, equivalent e-document, etc., and non- digital modes such
as obtaining copy of OVD certified by additional certifying authorities as allowed
for NRIs and PIOs. The AIFI shall undertake the following EDD measures for
non-face-to-face customer onboarding (other than customer onboarding in terms
of paragraph 25):
(1) If the AIFI has introduced the process of V-CIP, it shall provide the same
as the first option to the customer for remote onboarding. It is reiterated that
the AIFI shall treat processes complying with prescribed standards and
procedures for V-CIP on par with face-to-face CIP for the purpose of these
Directions.
(2) In order to prevent frauds, alternate mobile numbers shall not be linked post
CDD with such accounts for transaction OTP, transaction updates, etc. The
AIFI shall permit transactions only from the mobile number used for account
opening. The AIFI shall have a Board-approved policy delineating a robust
process of due diligence for dealing with requests for change of registered
mobile number.
(3) Apart from obtaining the current address proof, the AIFI shall verify the
current address through positive confirmation before allowing operations in
the account. The AIFI may carry out the positive confirmation by means of
such as address verification letter, contact point verification, deliverables,
etc.
(4) The AIFI shall obtain PAN from the customer and the PAN shall be verified
from the verification facility of the issuing authority.
(5) First transaction in such accounts shall be a credit from existing KYC-
complied bank account of the customer.
(6) The AIFI shall categorise such customers as high-risk customers and shall
subject accounts opened in non-face-to-face mode to enhanced monitoring
until the identity of the customer is verified in face-to-face manner or
through V-CIP.

children.title: 44. Accounts of Politically Exposed Persons (PEPs)
children.text: 44. Accounts of Politically Exposed Persons (PEPs)

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
(1) The AIFI shall have the option of establishing a relationship with PEPs
(whether as customer or beneficial owner) provided that, apart from
performing normal customer due diligence:

children.title: (i) the AIFI has in place appropriate risk management systems to
children.text:
(i) the AIFI has in place appropriate risk management systems to
determine whether the customer or the beneficial owner is a PEP;

children.title: (ii) The AIFI shall take reasonable measures for establishing the source
children.text:
(ii) The AIFI shall take reasonable measures for establishing the source
of funds / wealth;

children.title: (iii) the AIFI shall obtain approval to open an account for a PEP from the
children.text:
(iii) the AIFI shall obtain approval to open an account for a PEP from the
senior management;

children.title: (iv) the AIFI subjects all such accounts to enhanced monitoring on an on-
children.text:
(iv) the AIFI subjects all such accounts to enhanced monitoring on an on-
going basis;

children.title: (v) in the event of an existing customer or the beneficial owner of an
children.text:
(v) in the event of an existing customer or the beneficial owner of an
existing account subsequently becoming a PEP, the AIFI obtains the
senior management's approval to continue the business relationship;
(2) These instructions shall also apply to family members or close associates of
PEPs.

children.title: Explanation: For the purpose of this paragraph, 'Politically Exposed Persons'
children.text:
Explanation: For the purpose of this paragraph, 'Politically Exposed Persons'
(PEPs) are individuals who are or have been entrusted with prominent public
functions by a foreign country, including the Heads of States /
Governments, senior politicians, senior government or judicial or military
officers, senior executives of state-owned corporations and important political
party officials.

children.title: 45. Client accounts opened by professional intermediaries: The AIFI shall ensure
children.text:
45. Client accounts opened by professional intermediaries: The AIFI shall ensure
while opening client accounts through professional intermediaries, that:
(1) The AIFI shall identify clients when a professional intermediary opens a
client account on behalf of a single client.
(2) The AIFI shall have option to hold 'pooled' accounts managed by
professional intermediaries on behalf of entities like mutual funds, pension
funds or other types of funds.
(3) The AIFI shall not open accounts of such professional intermediaries who

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
are bound by any client confidentiality which prohibits disclosure of the
client details to the AIFI.
(4) The AIFI shall identify all the beneficial owners where intermediaries do not
co-mingle funds at the level of the AIFI, and there are 'sub- accounts', each
of them attributable to a beneficial owner, or where such funds are co-
mingled at the level of the AIFI, the AIFI shall look for the beneficial owners.
(5) The AIFI shall, at their discretion, rely on the 'customer due diligence' (CDD)
done by an intermediary, provided that the intermediary is a regulated and
supervised entity and has adequate systems in place to comply with the
KYC requirements of the customers.
(6) The ultimate responsibility for knowing the customer lies with the AIFI.
F2. Simplified Due Diligence

children.title: 46. Simplified norms for Self Help Groups (SHGs):
children.text:
46. Simplified norms for Self Help Groups (SHGs):
(1) The AIFI shall not require CDD of all the members of an SHG while opening
the savings bank account of the SHG.
(2) The CDD of all the office bearers shall suffice.
(3) The AIFI may undertake CDD of all the members of an SHG at the time of
credit linking of SHGs.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: 47. The AIFI shall take the following steps regarding maintenance, preservation and
children.text:
47. The AIFI shall take the following steps regarding maintenance, preservation and
reporting of customer information, with reference to provisions of PML Act and
Rules. The AIFI shall,
(1) maintain all necessary records of transactions between the AIFI and the
customer, both domestic and international, for at least five years from the
date of transaction;
(2) preserve the records pertaining to the identification of the customers and
their addresses, obtained while opening the account and during the course
of business relationship, for at least five years after the business
relationship has ended;
(3) swiftly make available, the identification records and transaction data to the
competent authorities upon request;
(4) introduce a system of maintaining proper records of transactions prescribed
under Rule 3 of Prevention of Money Laundering (Maintenance of Records)
Rules, 2005 (PML Rules, 2005);
(5) maintain all necessary information in respect of transactions prescribed
under PML Rule 3 to permit the reconstruction of an individual transaction,
including the following:

children.title: (i) the nature of the transactions;
children.text: (i) the nature of the transactions;

children.title: (ii) the amount of the transaction and the currency in which it was
children.text:
(ii) the amount of the transaction and the currency in which it was
denominated;

children.title: (iii) the date on which the transaction was conducted; and
children.text: (iii) the date on which the transaction was conducted; and

children.title: (iv) the parties to the transaction.
children.text:
(iv) the parties to the transaction.
(6) evolve a system for proper maintenance and preservation of account
information in a manner that allows the AIFI to retrieve data easily and
quickly whenever required or when competent authorities request it;
(7) maintain records of the identity and address of its customers, and records
in respect of transactions referred to in Rule 3 in hard or soft format.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: Explanation: For the purpose of this paragraph, the expressions 'records
children.text:
Explanation: For the purpose of this paragraph, the expressions 'records
pertaining to the identification', 'identification records', etc., shall
include updated records of the identification data, account files,
business correspondence and results of any analysis undertaken.

children.title: 48. The AIFI shall ensure that in case of customers who are non-profit organisations,
children.text:
48. The AIFI shall ensure that in case of customers who are non-profit organisations,
the AIFI registers details of such customers on the DARPAN Portal of NITI
Aayog. If they are not registered, the AIFI shall register the details on the
DARPAN Portal. The AIFI shall also maintain such registration records for a
period of five years after the business relationship between the customer and the
AIFI has ended or the account has been closed, whichever is later.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

hierarchy.title: Chapter VIII - Reporting Requirements to Financial Intelligence Unit - India
hierarchy.text: Chapter VIII - Reporting Requirements to Financial Intelligence Unit - India

children.title: 49. The AIFI shall furnish to the Director, Financial Intelligence Unit-India (FIU-IND),
children.text:
49. The AIFI shall furnish to the Director, Financial Intelligence Unit-India (FIU-IND),
the information referred to in Rule 3 of the PML (Maintenance of Records) Rules,
2005 in accordance with Rule 7 thereof.

children.title: Explanation: In terms of Third Amendment Rules notified September 22, 2015
children.text:
Explanation: In terms of Third Amendment Rules notified September 22, 2015
regarding amendment to sub rule 3 and 4 of rule 7, Director, FIU-IND shall have
powers to issue guidelines to the REs for detecting transactions referred to in
various clauses of sub-rule (1) of rule 3, to direct them about the form of
furnishing information and to specify the procedure and the manner of furnishing
information.

children.title: 50. The AIFI shall take note of the reporting formats and comprehensive reporting
children.text:
50. The AIFI shall take note of the reporting formats and comprehensive reporting
format guide, prescribed / released by FIU-IND and Report Generation Utility and
Report Validation Utility developed to assist reporting entities in the preparation
of prescribed reports. The AIFI which is yet to install / adopt suitable technological
tools for extracting CTR / STR from its live transaction data shall make use of the
editable electronic utilities to file electronic Cash Transaction Reports (CTR) /
Suspicious Transaction Reports (STR) which FIU- IND has placed on its website.
The Principal Officer of the AIFI, shall have suitable arrangement to cull out the
transaction details from branches which are yet to be computerised and to feed
the data into an electronic file with the help of the editable electronic utilities of
CTR / STR as have been made available by FIU-IND on its website
http://fiuindia.gov.in.

children.title: 51. When furnishing information to the Director, FIU-IND, a delay of each day in not
children.text:
51. When furnishing information to the Director, FIU-IND, a delay of each day in not
reporting a transaction or delay of each day in rectifying a mis-represented
transaction beyond the time limit as specified in the Rule shall constitute as a
separate violation. The AIFI shall not put any restriction on operations in the
accounts merely on the basis of the STR filed.
The AIFI, its directors, officers, and all employees shall ensure that the fact of
maintenance of records referred to in rule 3 of the PML (Maintenance of Records)
Rules, 2005, and furnishing of the information to the Director is confidential.
However, such confidentiality requirement shall not inhibit sharing of information
under paragraph 8 of these Directions of any analysis of transactions and

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
activities which appear unusual, if the AIFI has done any such analysis.

children.title: 52. To identify and report suspicious transactions effectively, the AIFI shall
children.text:
52. To identify and report suspicious transactions effectively, the AIFI shall
implement robust software that generates alerts when transactions are
inconsistent with a customer's risk categorisation and updated profile.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

hierarchy.title: Chapter IX - Requirements/obligations under International Agreements -
hierarchy.text:
Chapter IX - Requirements/obligations under International Agreements -
Communications from International Agencies

children.title: 53. Obligations under the Unlawful Activities (Prevention) (UAPA) Act, 1967:
children.text:
53. Obligations under the Unlawful Activities (Prevention) (UAPA) Act, 1967:
(1) The AIFI shall ensure that in terms of section 51A of the Unlawful Activities
(Prevention) (UAPA) Act, 1967 and amendments thereto, it does not have
any account in the name of individuals / entities appearing in the lists of
individuals and entities, suspected of having terrorist links, which are
approved by and periodically circulated by the United Nations Security
Council (UNSC). The details of the two lists are as under:

children.title: (i) The 'ISIL (Da'esh) and Al-Qaida Sanctions List', established and
children.text:
(i) The 'ISIL (Da'esh) and Al-Qaida Sanctions List', established and
maintained pursuant to Security Council resolutions 1267 / 1989 /
2253, which includes names of individuals and entities associated with
Al-Qaida, is available at
www.un.org/securitycouncil/sanctions/1267/aq_sanctions_list

children.title: (ii) The 'Taliban Sanctions List', established and maintained pursuant to
children.text:
(ii) The 'Taliban Sanctions List', established and maintained pursuant to
Security Council resolution 1988 (2011), which includes names of
individuals and entities associated with the Taliban is available at
https://www.un.org/securitycouncil/sanctions/1988/materials
(2) The AIFI shall also ensure to refer to the lists as available in the Schedules
to the Prevention and Suppression of Terrorism (Implementation of Security
Council Resolutions) Order, 2007, as amended from time to time. The AIFI
shall verify the aforementioned lists, i.e., UNSC Sanctions Lists and lists as
available in the Schedules to the Prevention and Suppression of Terrorism
(Implementation of Security Council Resolutions) Order, 2007, as amended
from time to time, on a daily basis, and any modifications to the lists in terms
of additions, deletions or other changes shall be taken into account by the
AIFI for meticulous compliance.
(3) The AIFI shall report the details of accounts resembling any of the
individuals / entities in the lists to FIU-IND in addition to advising the
Ministry of Home Affairs (MHA) as required under UAPA notification dated
February 2, 2021 (Annex I of these Directions).

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
(4) Freezing of Assets under Section 51A of UAPA, 1967: The AIFI shall strictly follow
the procedure laid down in the UAPA Order dated February 2, 2021 (Annex I of
these Directions), and shall ensure the meticulous compliance with the Order
issued by the Government. The list of Nodal Officers for UAPA is available on
the website of MHA.

children.title: 54. Obligations under Weapons of Mass Destruction (WMD) and their Delivery
children.text:
54. Obligations under Weapons of Mass Destruction (WMD) and their Delivery
Systems (Prohibition of Unlawful Activities) Act, 2005 (WMD Act, 2005):
(1) The AIFI shall ensure meticulous compliance with the 'Procedure for
Implementation of Section 12A of the Weapons of Mass Destruction (WMD)
and their Delivery Systems (Prohibition of Unlawful Activities) Act, 2005'
laid down in terms of section 12A of the WMD Act, 2005 vide Order dated
September 1, 2023, by the Ministry of Finance, Government of India (Annex
II of these Directions).
(2) In accordance with paragraph 3 of the aforementioned Order, the AIFI shall
ensure not to carry out transactions in case the particulars of the individual
/ entity match with the particulars in the designated list.
(3) Further, the AIFI shall run a check, on the given parameters, at the time of
establishing a relation with a customer and on a periodic basis to verify
whether individuals and entities in the designated list are holding any funds,
financial asset, etc., in the form of bank account, etc.
(4) In case of a match in the above cases, the AIFI shall immediately inform
the transaction details with full particulars of the funds, financial assets or
economic resources involved to the Central Nodal Officer (CNO),
designated as the authority to exercise powers under section 12A of the
WMD Act, 2005. The AIFI shall send a copy of the communication to the
State Nodal Officer, where the account / transaction is held and to the RBI.
(5) The AIFI may note that, in terms of Paragraph 1 of the Order, Director, FIU-
India has been designated as the CNO.
(6) The AIFI may refer to the designated list, as amended from time to time,
available on the portal of FIU-India.
(7) In case there are reasons to believe beyond doubt that funds or assets held

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
by a customer would fall under the purview of clause (a) or (b) of sub-
section (2) of section 12A of the WMD Act, 2005, the AIFI shall prevent
such individual / entity from conducting financial transactions, under
intimation to the CNO by email, FAX and by post, without delay.
(8) In case the AIFI receives an order to freeze assets under section 12A from
the CNO, the AIFI shall, without delay, take necessary action to comply with
the Order.
(9) The AIFI shall observe the process of unfreezing of funds, etc., as per
paragraph Seven of the Order. Accordingly, the AIFI shall forward a copy
of application received from an individual / entity regarding unfreezing,
along with full details of the asset frozen, as given by the applicant, to the
CNO by email, FAX and by post, within two working days.

children.title: 55. The AIFI shall verify every day, the 'UNSCR 1718 Sanctions List of Designated
children.text:
55. The AIFI shall verify every day, the 'UNSCR 1718 Sanctions List of Designated
Individuals and Entities', as available at https://www.mea.gov.in/Implementation-
of-UNSC-Sanctions-DPRK.htm, to take into account any modifications to the list
in terms of additions, deletions or other changes and also ensure compliance with
the 'Implementation of Security Council Resolution on Democratic People's
Republic of Korea Order, 2017', as amended from time to time by the Central
Government.

children.title: 56. In addition to the above, the AIFI shall take into account:
children.text:
56. In addition to the above, the AIFI shall take into account:
(1) other UNSCRs and
(2) lists in the first schedule and the fourth schedule of UAPA, 1967 and any
amendments to the same for compliance with the Government orders on
implementation of section 51A of the UAPA and section 12A of the WMD
Act.

children.title: 57. The AIFI shall undertake countermeasures when called upon so to do by any
children.text:
57. The AIFI shall undertake countermeasures when called upon so to do by any
international or intergovernmental organisation of which India is a member and
which is accepted by the Central Government.

children.title: 58. Jurisdictions that do not or insufficiently apply the FATF Recommendations:
children.text:
58. Jurisdictions that do not or insufficiently apply the FATF Recommendations:
(1) The AIFI shall consider the FATF Statements circulated by the RBI from
time to time, and publicly available information, for identifying countries,

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
which do not or insufficiently apply the FATF Recommendations. The AIFI
shall apply enhanced due diligence measures, which are effective and
proportionate to the risks, to business relationships and transactions with
natural and legal persons (including financial institutions) from countries for
which this is called for by the FATF.
(2) The AIFI shall give special attention to business relationships and
transactions with persons (including legal persons and other financial
institutions) from or in countries that do not or insufficiently apply the FATF
Recommendations and jurisdictions included in FATF Statements.

children.title: Explanation: The processes referred to in (1) and (2) above do not preclude
children.text:
Explanation: The processes referred to in (1) and (2) above do not preclude
the AIFI from having legitimate trade and business transactions with the
countries and jurisdictions mentioned in the FATF statement.
(3) The AIFI shall examine the background and purpose of transactions with
persons (including legal persons and other financial institutions) from
jurisdictions included in FATF Statements and countries that do not or
insufficiently apply the FATF Recommendations, retain written findings
together with all documents, and make them available to the RBI / other
relevant authorities, on request.

children.title: 59. The AIFI is encouraged to leverage latest technological innovations and tools for
children.text:
59. The AIFI is encouraged to leverage latest technological innovations and tools for
effective implementation of name screening to meet the sanctions requirements.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: 60. Secrecy Obligations and Sharing of Information:
children.text:
60. Secrecy Obligations and Sharing of Information:
(1) The AIFI shall maintain secrecy regarding the customer information that
arises out of the contractual relationship between the AIFI and the
customer.
(2) The AIFI shall treat information collected from customers for the purpose of
opening of account as confidential and shall not divulge details thereof for
the purpose of cross-selling, or for any other purpose without the express
permission of the customer.
(3) While considering the requests for data / information from Government and
other agencies, the AIFI shall satisfy itself that the information being sought
is not of such a nature as will violate the provisions of the laws relating to
secrecy in the transactions.
(4) The exceptions to the said rule shall be as under:

children.title: (i) Where disclosure is under compulsion of law,
children.text: (i) Where disclosure is under compulsion of law,

children.title: (ii) Where there is a duty to the public to disclose,
children.text: (ii) Where there is a duty to the public to disclose,

children.title: (iii) Where the interest of the AIFI requires disclosure, and
children.text: (iii) Where the interest of the AIFI requires disclosure, and

children.title: (iv) Where the disclosure is made with the express or implied consent of
children.text:
(iv) Where the disclosure is made with the express or implied consent of
the customer.

children.title: 61. Compliance with the provisions of Foreign Contribution (Regulation) Act, 2010:
children.text:
61. Compliance with the provisions of Foreign Contribution (Regulation) Act, 2010:
The AIFI shall ensure adherence to the provisions of Foreign Contribution
(Regulation) Act, 2010, and Rules made thereunder. Further, the AIFI shall also
ensure meticulous compliance with any instructions / communications on the
matter issued from time to time by the RBI based on advice received from the
Ministry of Home Affairs, Government of India.

children.title: 62. CDD Procedure and sharing KYC information with Central KYC Records
children.text:
62. CDD Procedure and sharing KYC information with Central KYC Records
Registry (CKYCR)
(1) Government of India has authorised the Central Registry of Securitisation
Asset Reconstruction and Security Interest of India (CERSAI), to act as, and
to perform the functions of the CKYCR vide Gazette Notification No. S.O.

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
3183(E) dated November 26, 2015.
(2) In terms of provision of Rule 9(1A) of the PML Rules, the AIFI shall capture
customer's KYC records and upload onto CKYCR within 10 days of
commencement of an account-based relationship with the customer.
(3) Operational Guidelines for uploading the KYC data have been released by
CERSAI.
(4) The AIFI shall capture the KYC information for sharing with the CKYCR in
the manner mentioned in the Rules, as per the KYC templates prepared for
'Individuals' and 'Legal Entities' (LEs), as the case may be. The templates
may be revised from time to time, as may be required and released by
CERSAI.
(5) The 'live run' of the CKYCR started from July 15, 2016, in a phased manner,
beginning with new 'individual accounts'. The AIFI shall upload KYC
records pertaining to accounts of LEs opened on or after April 1, 2021, with
CKYCR in terms of the provisions of the Rules ibid. The AIFI shall upload
KYC records as per the LE Template released by CERSAI.
(6) Once KYC Identifier is generated by CKYCR, the AIFI shall ensure that the
same is communicated to the LE as the case may be.
(7) In order to ensure that all KYC records are incrementally uploaded on to
CKYCR, the AIFI shall upload / update the KYC data pertaining to accounts of
LEs opened prior to the above-mentioned dates as per clauses (5) and (6),
respectively, at the time of periodic updation as specified in paragraph 40
of these Directions, or earlier, when the updated KYC information is
obtained / received from the customer. Also, whenever the AIFI obtains
additional or updated information from any customer as per clause (10)
below in this paragraph or Rule 9 (1C) of the PML Rules, the AIFI shall
within seven days or within such period as may be notified by the Central
Government, furnish the updated information to CKYCR, which shall update
the KYC records of the existing customer in CKYCR. CKYCR shall
thereafter inform electronically all the reporting entities who have dealt with
the concerned customer regarding updation of KYC record of the said
customer. Once CKYCR informs the AIFI regarding an update in the KYC

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
record of an existing customer, the AIFI shall retrieve the updated KYC
records from CKYCR and update the KYC record maintained by the AIFI.
(8) The AIFI shall ensure that during periodic updation, the customers are
migrated to the current CDD standard.
(9) For the purpose of establishing an account-based relationship, updation /
periodic updation or for verification of identity of a customer, the AIFI shall seek
the KYC Identifier from the customer or retrieve the KYC Identifier, if
available, from the CKYCR and proceed to obtain KYC records online by
using such KYC Identifier and shall not require a customer to submit the
same KYC records or information or any other additional identification
documents or details, unless-

children.title: (i) there is a change in the information of the customer as existing in the
children.text:
(i) there is a change in the information of the customer as existing in the
records of CKYCR; or

children.title: (ii) the KYC record or information retrieved is incomplete or is not as per
children.text:
(ii) the KYC record or information retrieved is incomplete or is not as per
the current applicable KYC norms; or

children.title: (iii) the validity period of downloaded documents has lapsed; or
children.text: (iii) the validity period of downloaded documents has lapsed; or

children.title: (iv) the AIFI considers it necessary in order to verify the identity or address
children.text:
(iv) the AIFI considers it necessary in order to verify the identity or address
(including current address) of the customer, or to perform enhanced
due diligence or to build an appropriate risk profile of the customer.
[1Explanation: The RE that has last uploaded or updated the customer's
KYC records in the CKYCR shall be responsible for verifying the identity and
/ or address of the customer, as applicable. Accordingly, any AIFI
downloading and relying on such records from the CKCYR shall not be
required to re-verify the authenticity of the customer's identity and / or
address, provided the KYC records downloaded from CKYCR are current
and compliant with the PML Act, 2002 / PML Rules, 2005. The AIFI
downloading and relying on KYC records downloaded from the CKCYR
shall remain responsible for all aspects of CDD procedure and provisions of
these Directions, except verification of identity and / or address of the
1 Inserted with effect from December 29, 2025 vide Reserve Bank of India (All India Financial Institutions - Know
Your Customer) Amendment Directions, 2025 dated December 29, 2025

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
customer.]

children.title: 63. Reporting requirement under Foreign Account Tax Compliance Act
children.text:
63. Reporting requirement under Foreign Account Tax Compliance Act
(FATCA) and Common Reporting Standards (CRS): Under FATCA and CRS,
the AIFI shall adhere to the provisions of Income Tax Rules 114F, 114G and
114H and determine whether they are a Reporting Financial Institution as defined
in Income Tax Rule 114F and if so, shall take following steps for complying with
the reporting requirements:
(1) Register on the related e-filing portal of Income Tax Department as
Reporting Financial Institutions at the link
https://incometaxindiaefiling.gov.in/ post login -> My Account --> Register
as Reporting Financial Institution.
(2) Submit online reports by using the digital signature of the 'Designated
Director' by either uploading the Form 61B or 'NIL' report, for which, the
schema prepared by Central Board of Direct Taxes (CBDT) shall be
referred to.

children.title: Explanation: REs shall refer to the spot reference rates published by
children.text:
Explanation: REs shall refer to the spot reference rates published by
Foreign Exchange Dealers' Association of India (FEDAI) on their website at
http://www.fedai.org.in/RevaluationRates.aspx for carrying out the due
diligence procedure for the purposes of identifying reportable accounts in
terms of Rule 114H.
(3) Develop Information Technology (IT) framework for carrying out due
diligence procedure and for recording and maintaining the same, as
provided in Rule 114H.
(4) Develop a system of audit for the IT framework and compliance with Rules
114F, 114G and 114H of Income Tax Rules.
(5) Constitute a 'High-Level Monitoring Committee' under the Designated
Director or any other equivalent functionary to ensure compliance.
(6) Ensure compliance with updated instructions / rules / guidance notes /
Press releases / issued on the subject by Central Board of Direct Taxes
(CBDT) from time to time and available on the website
http://www.incometaxindia.gov.in/Pages/default.aspx. REs may take note

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
of the following:

children.title: (i) updated Guidance Note on FATCA and CRS
children.text: (i) updated Guidance Note on FATCA and CRS

children.title: (ii) a press release on 'Closure of Financial Accounts' under Rule 114H(8)
children.text: (ii) a press release on 'Closure of Financial Accounts' under Rule 114H(8)

children.title: 64. Operation of Accounts and Money Mules: The AIFI shall strictly adhere to the
children.text:
64. Operation of Accounts and Money Mules: The AIFI shall strictly adhere to the
instructions on opening of accounts and monitoring of transactions, to minimise
the operations of 'Money Mules' that are used to launder the proceeds of fraud
schemes (e.g., phishing and identity theft) by criminals who gain illegal access
to deposit accounts by recruiting third parties that act as 'money mules'. The AIFI
shall undertake diligence measures and meticulous monitoring to identify
accounts which are operated as Money Mules and take appropriate action,
including reporting of suspicious transactions to FIU-IND. Further, if it is
established that an account opened and operated is that of a Money Mule, but
STR was not filed by the concerned AIFI, it shall then be deemed that the AIFI
has not complied with these directions.

children.title: 65. The AIFI shall allot Unique Customer Identification Code (UCIC) while entering
children.text:
65. The AIFI shall allot Unique Customer Identification Code (UCIC) while entering
into new relationships with individual customers as also the existing individual
customers.

children.title: 66. The AIFI shall, at their option, not issue UCIC to all walk-in / occasional
children.text:
66. The AIFI shall, at their option, not issue UCIC to all walk-in / occasional
customers provided it is ensured that there is adequate mechanism to identify
such walk-in customers who have frequent transactions with them and ensure
that they are allotted UCIC.

children.title: 67. Introduction of New Technologies: The AIFI shall identify and assess the ML
children.text:
67. Introduction of New Technologies: The AIFI shall identify and assess the ML
/ TF risks that may arise in relation to the development of new products and new
business practices, including new delivery mechanisms, and the use of new or
developing technologies for both new and existing products.
Further, the AIFI shall ensure:
(1) to undertake the ML / TF risk assessments prior to the launch or use of
such products, practices, services, technologies; and
(2) adoption of a risk-based approach to manage and mitigate the risks through
appropriate EDD measures and transaction monitoring, etc.

children.title: 68. Correspondent Banking: The AIFI shall have a policy approved by its Boards,
children.text: 68. Correspondent Banking: The AIFI shall have a policy approved by its Boards,

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
or by a committee headed by the Chairman / CEO / MD, to lay down parameters
for approving cross-border correspondent banking and other similar
relationships. In addition to performing normal CDD measures, such
relationships shall be subject to the following conditions:
(1) The AIFI shall gather sufficient information about a respondent bank to
understand fully the nature of the respondent bank's business and to
determine from publicly available information the reputation of the
respondent bank and the quality of supervision, including whether it has
been subjected to a ML / TF investigation or regulatory action. The AIFI
shall assess the respondent bank's AML / CFT controls.
(2) The information gathered in relation to the nature of business of the
respondent bank shall include information on management, major business
activities, purpose of opening the account, identity of any third-party entities
that will use the correspondent banking services, regulatory / supervisory
framework in the respondent bank's home country among other relevant
information.
(3) The AIFI shall obtain the prior approval from senior management for
establishing new correspondent banking relationships. However, the AIFI
shall also take post facto-approval of the Board or the Committee
empowered for this purpose.
(4) The AIFI shall clearly document and understand the respective AML / CFT
responsibilities of institutions involved.
(5) In the case of payable-through-accounts, the correspondent AIFI shall be
satisfied that the respondent bank has conducted CDD on the customers
having direct access to the accounts of the correspondent AIFI and is
undertaking ongoing 'due diligence' on them.
(6) The correspondent AIFI shall ensure that the respondent bank is able to
provide the relevant CDD information immediately on request.
(7) The AIFI shall not enter into or continue a correspondent relationship with
a shell bank.
(8) The AIFI shall ensure that the respondent banks do not permit their

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
accounts to be used by shell banks.
(9) The AIFI shall be cautious of correspondent banking relationships with
institutions located in jurisdictions which have strategic deficiencies or have
not made sufficient progress in implementation of FATF
Recommendations.
(10) The AIFI shall ensure that respondent banks have KYC / AML policies and
procedures in place and apply enhanced 'due diligence' procedures for
transactions carried out through the correspondent accounts.

children.title: 69. Wire Transfer
children.text:
69. Wire Transfer
(1) Information requirements for wire transfers for the purpose of this Direction:

children.title: (i) All cross-border wire transfers shall be accompanied by accurate,
children.text:
(i) All cross-border wire transfers shall be accompanied by accurate,
complete, and meaningful originator and beneficiary information as
mentioned below:

children.title: (a) name of the originator;
children.text: (a) name of the originator;

children.title: (b) the originator account number where such an account is used to
children.text:
(b) the originator account number where such an account is used to
process the transaction;

children.title: (c) the originator's address, or national identity number, or customer
children.text:
(c) the originator's address, or national identity number, or customer
identification number, or date and place of birth;

children.title: (d) name of the beneficiary; and
children.text: (d) name of the beneficiary; and

children.title: (e) the beneficiary account number where such an account is used to
children.text:
(e) the beneficiary account number where such an account is used to
process the transaction.
In the absence of an account, a unique transaction reference number
should be included which permits traceability of the transaction.

children.title: (ii) In case of batch transfer, where several individual cross-border wire
children.text:
(ii) In case of batch transfer, where several individual cross-border wire
transfers from a single originator are bundled in a batch file for
transmission to beneficiaries, they (i.e., individual transfers) are
exempted from the requirements of clause (i) above in respect of
originator information, provided that they include the originator's
account number or unique transaction reference number, as mentioned
above, and the batch file contains required and accurate originator

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
information, and full beneficiary information, that is fully traceable within
the beneficiary country.

children.title: (iii) Domestic wire transfer, where the originator is an account holder of the
children.text:
(iii) Domestic wire transfer, where the originator is an account holder of the
ordering AIFI, shall be accompanied by originator and beneficiary
information, as indicated for cross-border wire transfers in (i) and (ii)
above.

children.title: (iv) Domestic wire transfers of ₹50,000 and above, where the originator is
children.text:
(iv) Domestic wire transfers of ₹50,000 and above, where the originator is
not an account holder of the ordering AIFI, shall also be
accompanied by originator and beneficiary information as indicated for
cross-border wire transfers.
In case of domestic wire transfers below ₹50,000 where the originator
is not an account holder of the ordering AIFIs and where the information
accompanying the wire transfer can be made available to the
beneficiary RE and appropriate authorities by other means, it is
sufficient for the ordering AIFI to include a unique transaction reference
number, provided that this number or identifier will permit the
transaction to be traced back to the originator or the beneficiary.
The ordering AIFI shall make the information available within three
working / business days of receiving the request from the intermediary
RE, beneficiary RE, or from appropriate competent authorities.

children.title: (v) The AIFI shall ensure that all the information on the wire transfers shall
children.text:
(v) The AIFI shall ensure that all the information on the wire transfers shall
be immediately made available to appropriate law enforcement
authorities, prosecuting / competent authorities as well as FIU-IND on
receiving such requests with appropriate level provisions.

children.title: (vi) The wire transfer instructions are not intended to cover the following
children.text:
(vi) The wire transfer instructions are not intended to cover the following
types of payments:

children.title: (a) Any transfer that flows from a transaction carried out using a credit
children.text:
(a) Any transfer that flows from a transaction carried out using a credit
card / debit card / Prepaid Payment Instrument (PPI), including
through a token or any other similar reference string associated
with the card / PPI, for the purchase of goods or services, so long
as the credit or debit card number or PPI id or reference number
accompanies all transfers flowing from the transaction. However,

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
when a credit or debit card or PPI is used as a payment system to
effect a person-to-person wire transfer, the wire transfer
instructions shall apply to such transactions and the necessary
information should be included in the message.

children.title: (b) Financial institution-to-financial institution transfers and
children.text:
(b) Financial institution-to-financial institution transfers and
settlements, where both the originator person and the beneficiary
person are regulated financial institutions acting on their own
behalf.
It is, however, clarified that nothing within these instructions will impact
the obligation of the AIFI to comply with applicable reporting
requirements under PML Act, 2002, and the Rules made thereunder, or
any other statutory requirement in force.
(2) Responsibilities of ordering AIFI, intermediary AIFI and beneficiary AIFI, effecting
wire transfer, are as under:

children.title: (i) Ordering AIFI:
children.text: (i) Ordering AIFI:

children.title: (a) The ordering AIFI shall ensure that all cross-border and qualifying
children.text:
(a) The ordering AIFI shall ensure that all cross-border and qualifying
domestic wire transfers {viz., transactions as per clauses (iii) and (iv)
of clause (1) above}, contain required and accurate originator
information and required beneficiary information, as indicated above.

children.title: (b) Customer Identification shall be made if a customer, who is not an
children.text:
(b) Customer Identification shall be made if a customer, who is not an
account holder of the ordering AIFI, is intentionally structuring
domestic wire transfers below ₹50,000 to avoid reporting or
monitoring. In case of non-cooperation from the customer, efforts shall
be made to establish identity and if the same transaction is found to
be suspicious, STR may be filed with FIU-IND in accordance with the
PML Rules.

children.title: (c) Ordering AIFI shall not execute the wire transfer if it is not able to
children.text:
(c) Ordering AIFI shall not execute the wire transfer if it is not able to
comply with the requirements stipulated in this paragraph.

children.title: (ii) Intermediary AIFI:
children.text: (ii) Intermediary AIFI:

children.title: (a) The AIFI processing an intermediary element of a chain of wire
children.text:
(a) The AIFI processing an intermediary element of a chain of wire
transfers shall ensure that all originator and beneficiary information

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
accompanying a wire transfer is retained with the transfer.

children.title: (b) Where technical limitations prevent the required originator or
children.text:
(b) Where technical limitations prevent the required originator or
beneficiary information accompanying a cross-border wire transfer
from remaining with a related domestic wire transfer, the intermediary
AIFI shall keep a record, for at least five years, of all the information
received from the ordering financial institution or another intermediary
RE.

children.title: (c) Intermediary AIFI shall take reasonable measures to identify cross-
children.text:
(c) Intermediary AIFI shall take reasonable measures to identify cross-
border wire transfers that lack required originator information or
required beneficiary information. Such measures should be consistent
with straight-through processing.

children.title: (d) Intermediary AIFI shall have effective risk-based policies and
children.text:
(d) Intermediary AIFI shall have effective risk-based policies and
procedures for determining:
a. when to execute, reject, or suspend a wire transfer lacking
required originator or required beneficiary information; and
b. the appropriate follow-up action including seeking further
information and if the transaction is found to be suspicious,
reporting to FIU-IND in accordance with the PML Rules.

children.title: (iii) Beneficiary AIFI:
children.text: (iii) Beneficiary AIFI:

children.title: (a) Beneficiary AIFI shall take reasonable measures, including post-event
children.text:
(a) Beneficiary AIFI shall take reasonable measures, including post-event
monitoring or real-time monitoring where feasible, to identify cross-
border wire transfers and qualifying domestic wire transfers {viz.,
transactions as per clauses (iii) and (iv) of clause (1) above}, that lack
required originator information or required beneficiary information.

children.title: (b) Beneficiary AIFI shall have effective risk-based policies and
children.text:
(b) Beneficiary AIFI shall have effective risk-based policies and
procedures for determining: (a) when to execute, reject, or suspend a
wire transfer lacking required originator or required beneficiary
information; and (b) the appropriate follow-up action follow-up action
including seeking further information and if the transaction is found to
be suspicious, reporting to FIU-IND in accordance with the PML
Rules.

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
(3) Other Obligations:

children.title: (i) Obligations in respect of the AIFI's engagement or involvement with
children.text:
(i) Obligations in respect of the AIFI's engagement or involvement with
unregulated entities in the process of wire transfer: The AIFI shall be
cognizant of their obligations under these instructions and ensure strict
compliance, in respect of engagement or involvement of any unregulated
entities in the process of wire transfer. More specifically, whenever there is
involvement of any unregulated entities in the process of wire transfers, the
concerned AIFI shall be fully responsible for information, reporting and
other requirements and therefore shall ensure, inter alia, that:

children.title: (a) there is unhindered flow of complete wire transfer information, as
(b) the agreement / arrangement, if any, with such unregulated entities by
children.text:
(b) the agreement / arrangement, if any, with such unregulated entities by
the AIFI clearly stipulates the obligations under wire transfer
instructions; and

children.title: (c) a termination clause is available in their agreement / arrangement, if
children.text:
(c) a termination clause is available in their agreement / arrangement, if
any, with such entities so that in case the unregulated entities are
unable to support the wire information requirements, the agreement /
arrangement can be terminated. Existing agreements / arrangements,
if any, with such entities shall be reviewed within three months to
ensure aforementioned requirements.

children.title: (ii) The AIFI's responsibility while undertaking cross-border wire transfer with
children.text:
(ii) The AIFI's responsibility while undertaking cross-border wire transfer with
respect to name screening (such that they do not process cross-border
transactions of designated persons and entities).
The AIFI is prohibited from conducting transactions with designated persons
and entities and accordingly, in addition to compliance with Chapter IX of
the Directions, the AIFI shall ensure that they do not process cross-border
transactions of designated persons and entities.

children.title: (iii) The AIFI's responsibility to fulfil record management requirements: Complete
children.text:
(iii) The AIFI's responsibility to fulfil record management requirements: Complete
originator and beneficiary information relating to wire transfers shall be
preserved by the AIFI involved in the wire transfer, in accordance with
paragraph 46 of these Directions.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: 70. Quoting of PAN: PAN or equivalent e-document thereof of customers shall be
children.text:
70. Quoting of PAN: PAN or equivalent e-document thereof of customers shall be
obtained and verified while undertaking transactions as per the provisions of
Income Tax Rule 114B applicable to AIFIs, as amended from time to time, which
includes opening of accounts with AIFIs, etc. Form 60 shall be obtained from
persons who do not have PAN or equivalent e-document thereof.

children.title: 71. Hiring of Employees and Employee training:
children.text:
71. Hiring of Employees and Employee training:
(1) The AIFI shall put in place an adequate screening mechanism, including
Know Your Employee / Staff policy, as an integral part of its personnel
recruitment / hiring process.
(2) The AIFI shall endeavour to ensure that the staff dealing with / being
deployed for KYC / AML / CFT matters have: high integrity and ethical
standards, good understanding of extant KYC / AML / CFT standards,
effective communication skills and ability to keep up with the changing KYC
/ AML / CFT landscape, nationally and internationally. The AIFI shall also
strive to develop an environment which fosters open communication and
high integrity amongst the staff.
(3) The AIFI shall put in place an on-going employee training programme so
that the members of staff are adequately trained in KYC / AML / CFT policy.
The focus of the training shall be different for frontline staff, compliance staff
and staff dealing with new customers. The AIFI shall specially train the front
desk staff to handle issues arising from lack of customer education. The
AIFI shall ensure the proper staffing of the audit function with persons
adequately trained and well-versed in KYC / AML / CFT policies of the AIFI,
regulation and related issues.

children.text: <image>

hierarchy.title: Chapter XI - Repeal and Other Provisions
hierarchy.text: Chapter XI - Repeal and Other Provisions

children.title: A. Repeal and saving
children.text: A. Repeal and saving

children.title: 72. With the issue of these Directions, the existing Directions, instructions, and
children.text:
72. With the issue of these Directions, the existing Directions, instructions, and
guidelines relating to Know Your Customer as applicable to All India Financial
Institutions stand repealed, as communicated vide circular
DOR.RRC.REC.302/33-01-010/2025-26 dated November 28, 2025. The
Directions, instructions, and guidelines repealed prior to the issuance of these
Directions shall continue to remain repealed.

children.title: 73. Notwithstanding such repeal, any action taken or purported to have been taken,
children.text:
73. Notwithstanding such repeal, any action taken or purported to have been taken,
or initiated under the repealed Directions, instructions, or guidelines shall
continue to be governed by the provisions thereof. All approvals or
acknowledgments granted under these repealed lists shall be deemed as
governed by these Directions. Further, the repeal of these directions,
instructions, or guidelines shall not in any way prejudicially affect:
(1) any right, obligation or liability acquired, accrued, or incurred thereunder;
(2) any penalty, forfeiture, or punishment incurred in respect of any contravention
committed thereunder;
(3) any investigation, legal proceeding, or remedy in respect of any such right,
privilege, obligation, liability, penalty, forfeiture, or punishment as aforesaid;
and any such investigation, legal proceedings or remedy may be instituted,
continued, or enforced and any such penalty, forfeiture or punishment may
be imposed as if those directions, instructions, or guidelines had not been
repealed.

children.title: B. Application of other laws not barred:
children.text: B. Application of other laws not barred:

children.title: 74. The provisions of these Directions shall be in addition to, and not in derogation
children.text:
74. The provisions of these Directions shall be in addition to, and not in derogation
of the provisions of any other laws, rules, regulations, or directions, for the time
being in force.

children.title: C. Interpretation:
children.text: C. Interpretation:

children.title: 75. For the purpose of giving effect to the provisions of these Directions or in order
children.text:
75. For the purpose of giving effect to the provisions of these Directions or in order
to remove any difficulties in the application or interpretation of the provisions of

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
these Directions, the RBI may, if it considers necessary, issue necessary

(Veena Srivastava)
